*CONFIDENTIAL*



Hi Nik,



February was a great month for Truemed. We shipped a new version of *our
website*
<https://www.getcabal.com/ahoy/messages/D01sL47PIasJuTyJQXhWChkq4o5gpVDU/click?signature=8542f27afeb919b7619fd79755adcb799418b626&url=https%3A%2F%2Ftruemed.com%2F>,
overhauled our *B2B pages*
<https://www.getcabal.com/ahoy/messages/D01sL47PIasJuTyJQXhWChkq4o5gpVDU/click?signature=a02c0d3a82a7442d87e778440a0cd8966bf0a456&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.truemed.com%2Fpartners>,
landed several new conversion experiments, signed new merchants, and made
tremendous progress towards some big deals I’ll announce next month.



That said, we could still use your help with hiring:
*🎯 Asks*

   1. If you have any talented *PMs or engineers* with e-commerce or
   payments experience, we’d love to chat with them.
   2. If you’ve worked with any exceptional *growth marketers*, please put
   me in touch!

🏦* KPIs*

   - Last month revenue: $3.28M (-1% to plan)
   - Cash balance: $38.6M
   - Last month EBITDA: -$300k
   - Current runway: 10+ years 😃



February was a traditionally lower month due to seasonality, but we still
managed to land within 1% of plan. We have a ton of cash in the bank and a
lot of runway to achieve our mission to make a dent on chronic disease.


*👍 Highlights*

Some highlights from February:

   1. We launched Garmin in Q4 (one of our largest merchants), and they
   were kind enough to mention us in their *2025 earnings call*

   From our contact at Garmin: *"in the 15 years I have been at Garmin, I
   have never seen an initiative be both implemented so quickly, and also so
   well received like Truemed."*
   He also said the CEO hand-picked Truem

updates.rawText:
*CONFIDENTIAL*



Hi Nik,



Happy 2026!



2025 was a big one for Truemed. Some highlights:

   1. We grew revenue 3x YoY, from $7M 2024 to $21M in 2025, while
   maintaining similar burn to 2024
   2. We stood up a medical corporation and invested a *ton* in our
   compliance and research infrastructure. We are now - by far - the player in
   this world with the best research and clinical rigor in the space,
   something that continues to win us deals with major merchant partners.
   3. We signed hundreds of massive merchants: Life Time Fitness, Barry's,
   Rogue, Garmin, Mattress Firm, and hundreds more.
   4. We facilitated 1M+ LMNs, and steered over $300M towards root-cause
   lifestyle interventions!

2026 will be a huge year for us. This year, we’re focused on scaling the
team, deepening our partnerships with many players in the space, and
solidifying ourselves as the clear leader in this world we are carving out.



This year, we get to focus on a few areas:

   1. *Truemed health *- our mission is to drive more dollars towards
   effective lifestyle interventions. Today, that means unlocking HSA/FSA
   dollars, but we want to do more. Our north star this year is driving $1
   from the traditional healthcare world towards one of our merchant partners.
   We have multiple ideas here (and a small team strictly focused on this
   goal), and will be testing 1-2 ideas per month over the next two quarters.
   2. *Partnerships* - this is the year we are going deeper with many of
   our partners. We’ll build deeper integrations, partner with large platforms
   (like ABC, Mindbody, etc), and make Truemed a default payment option
   anywhere users are spending money on root cause interventions.
   3. *Improve our product *- To that end, we’ll also improve our product.
   We’ve certainly got product-market fit, and spent most of last year
   building features in reaction to customer asks. This year, our goal is to
   make o

updates.rawText:
*CONFIDENTIAL*



Hi Nik,


Q4 is our Super Bowl at Truemed, as FSA funds expire in December and our
merchants move into a busy Black Friday, Cyber Monday (BFCM) season. In
October, we went live with Mattress Firm and Technogym, signed Just
Ingredients, MyFitnessPal, ButcherBox, and Thorne, and unblocked a major
issue with Fidelity that should lead to a ~4% conversion rate increase.



Additionally, we saw case studies with Canyon, Mattress Firm, and Hydrow
that definitively shows Truemed is driving an AOV lift of around 50% (!!!).
Pretty exciting, and these results are setting us up as key partners
leading into Q4/Q1 next year.
*🎯 Asks*

   1. We still have *8 open positions*
   <https://www.getcabal.com/ahoy/messages/QlGEJRrxkkIFeWIn9Q0aRaA8cHIwD3ui/click?signature=1cd32b5a85b67e57c82a33d3f943aeb24f9eb188&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.truemed.com%2Fcareers>
   across marketing and engineering. If you have any recommendations, let us
   know!

🏦* KPIs*

   - Last month revenue: ~$1.62M (+$0.04M to plan)
   - Cash balance: $37.6M
   - Burn: ~$530k last month
   - Current runway: ~6 years



We have been expanding the team to invest more aggressively in both
marketing and in new platform integrations, both which will unlock major
new revenue streams in the next 12-24 months. I’m very happy with our
current cash position and the way the team is coming together. 2026 should
be a big one!


*👍 Highlights*

Some highlights from October:

   1. We beat our aggressive plan by 2%, which sets us up well for a
   massive November/December. The last two years, we have seen a significant
   acceleration in Q4 due to seasonality, and expect to see the same in
   Nov/Dec this year.

   2. Mattress Firm is a massive company, and measured AOV for Truemed and
   non-Truemed orders. They found that *non-Truemed* AOV was $700, and
   Truemed AOV was $1400… a full 100% increase! Incredible, and case studies
   like this help us unlock deepe

updates.rawText:
*Note: The below is a timely message from Calley. Please expect a more
traditional investor update later this week on company performance.*


Hi there,

I am writing to share background on our mission and an update about me:



After my mother died in 2021, I decided to devote my life to fixing the
broken incentives of our medical system. As I dove into helping Casey write
Good Energy, I began posting blog posts about this mission. One of the
first people who reached out was Justin - and we began researching ideas.



We started the company to solve a problem: We are the sickest country in
the developed world, largely based on the incentives of our medical system.
We spend $5 trillion per year on healthcare, and 95% of it goes to medical
procedures after we get sick. To fix this, we need to steer medical
incentives towards interventions that actually prevent and reverse disease.
Because 90% of medical spending goes to managing chronic lifestyle
conditions, this means trillions of medical dollars must be steered towards
incentivizing better lifestyle conditions. Justin and I dreamed about
spending the next 20 years pushing on this generational objective of
changing the foundational incentives of our country’s largest industry. Our
hope was that maybe we could start a company that would be a platform to
work with people like Dr. Mark Hyman and elected leaders to change the
trajectory of health care policy.



The lightbulb for Truemed came when we found the legal definition of
medicine (“the costs of diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or
prevention of disease, and for the purpose of affecting any part or
function of the body”) and realized doctors should be prescribing much more
*true *medicine - and could do that through a telehealth platform. For many
of the diseases plaguing us, exercise is a clinically better intervention
than the drug treadmill we’re on.



Truemed’s solution struck a nerve almost immediately with c

updates.subject: Blox Snacks Investor Update: Q4/FY'25
summary: With all the 2026 category reviews complete, we expect FruitBlox TDPs to grow +9% this year, from approx. 20.5k to 22.5k TDPs; notable changes at Walmart (+8%) and Target (+125%).
Both Unspeakable items (FruitBlox and FruitBlox Sours) perform in the top-half of category velocities, and the Unspeakable FruitBlox Sours item is the #1 sours fruit snack in the category.
While the Unspeakable items perform well in the category, our two other items (FruitBlox Aphmau and FruitBlox Sours Stokes Twins) lag behind.
Additionally, we decided to completely exit the JuiceBlox business, so that is fully winding down this month.
updates.rawText:
Blox Investors,

This update is a bit later than when I'd normally send, but there's been a lot going on to start the year...including preparing to launch a NEW disruptive BFY brand in fruit snacks (read to the end!). Below are our Q4/FY'25 results, and what we we're bringing to the business in 2026.

FINANCIAL HIGHLIGHTS

In Q4'25, we achieved $2.4MM in Net Sales, bringing the FY'25 Net Sales total to $10MM (flat vs prior year). While Q4 was slightly profitable/break-even, the year closed out at a net loss of -$2.4MM, primarily driven by significant tariff expenses and a one-timer deposit write-off from our previous FruitBlox manufacturer. While we've been in a tight working capital position up to this point, we're fully operational at our North American-based FruitBlox manufacturer, which has alleviated our cash crunch and allowed us to build up a $1.2MM+ cash balance in Q1'26. Additionally, we've renewed our credit facility with Dwight Funding ($1.5MM), which remains untapped.

[cid:image001.png@01DCC6AC.CA834F70]


RETAIL UPDATE


  *   With all the 2026 category reviews complete, we expect FruitBlox TDPs to grow +9% this year, from approx. 20.5k to 22.5k TDPs; notable changes at Walmart (+8%) and Target (+125%).
  *   Both Unspeakable items (FruitBlox and FruitBlox Sours) perform in the top-half of category velocities, and the Unspeakable FruitBlox Sours item is the #1 sours fruit snack in the category.
  *   While the Unspeakable items perform well in the category, our two other items (FruitBlox Aphmau and FruitBlox Sours Stokes Twins) lag behind.
  *   Additionally, we decided to completely exit the JuiceBlox business, so that is fully winding down this month.
[cid:image002.png@01DCC6AC.CA834F70]


2026 PORTFOLIO UPDATE

In 2026, we are leaning into our top performing YouTube Creator IP (Unspeakable) and adding  a successful IP from Roblox (with Aphmau and Stokes Twins leaving the FruitBlox portfolio). Roblox now outpaces Minecraft and

FruitBlox Sours: Launched into retail (WMT, Kroger, HEB), with Unspeakable FruitBlox Sours as our #1 performing SKU most weeks
Velocities: We consistently have 2 items in the 1st or 2nd quartile in most retailers, with our 3rd & 4th items lagging slightly behind; we continue to work to elevate all SKUs in the portfolio
Manufacturing: We are now in production at our new fruit snacks manufacturer based in North America, and will see the improved unit economics flow into our financials in late Q3/early Q4
Team: The team is in a great spot, but we did have our first voluntary exit in 2.5 years of being in business, with our Head of Sales leaving Blox earlier this month.
ASK: We are actively recruiting for a new Head of Sales (Director/Sr Director level), and if you have anyone you'd recommend we talk to, please send them my way!

Branding: To make that launch (timing TBD, based upon product dev execution), we are pausing the rebrand work to SnackMode; along with product info, will share out branding plan as we finalize the launch

Fundraise: We will be in a much stronger position to fundraise in 2026, when we have more in-market results at Grocery (e.g., Kroger, Giant Eagle, etc.) and when we can showcase where we are headed from a product/innovation standpoint
updates.rawText:
Blox Snacks Investors,

I'm a little later than normal on my quarterly update, but we've been focused as a team on both our manufacturing transition and supporting new distribution. Below are our Q2'25 results, and some key updates on the business.

FINANCIAL HIGHLIGHTS

In Q2 we delivered a topline of $2.7MM in Net Sales, +40% vs. Q1, as we shipped into new retail distribution. Profitability remained challenged, as this quarter was again significantly impacted by tariffs ($570K paid in tariffs) and we wrote off a $560K deposit at our original manufacturer, GummiWorld (see below for Adj EBITDA removing those two line items). We assessed litigation vs. GummiWorld, but the legal costs associated with it and the timeline to recoup our money, made this an impractical path to pursue. Q3 will be our last quarter with tariffs, and they will be at a significantly lower level than Q2.

[cid:image001.png@01DC27D3.48B3FC10]


KEY BUSINESS UPDATES

  *   Retail Distribution: Expanded store count from 3,400 stores (WMT, TGT) to 6,500 stores, adding Kroger, HEB, Giant Eagle, Wakefern, and Dierbergs
  *   FruitBlox Sours: Launched into retail (WMT, Kroger, HEB), with Unspeakable FruitBlox Sours as our #1 performing SKU most weeks
  *   Velocities: We consistently have 2 items in the 1st or 2nd quartile in most retailers, with our 3rd & 4th items lagging slightly behind; we continue to work to elevate all SKUs in the portfolio
  *   Manufacturing: We are now in production at our new fruit snacks manufacturer based in North America, and will see the improved unit economics flow into our financials in late Q3/early Q4
  *   Team: The team is in a great spot, but we did have our first voluntary exit in 2.5 years of being in business, with our Head of Sales leaving Blox earlier this month.
     *   ASK: We are actively recruiting for a new Head of Sales (Director/Sr Director level), and if you have anyone you'd recommend we talk to, please send them my way!

STRATEG

updates.rawText:
BloxSnacks Investors,

The last few months have been unprecedented times in CPG. And to that end, the story of Q1'25 (into early Q2) for the Blox team was driven by tariffs. As I've shared before, getting the right long-term manufacturing partner in place has been a key priority for Blox. But tariff policy uncertainty to begin the year turned into unthinkable cost increases on our product coming from China as tariffs were raised numerous times (now at 175.6% on our product). And while the negative consequences of this policy will be seen in Blox financials into Q3'25, we now have a manufacturing partnership in place that will put us in the best position in our company's history. This will be the financial foundation for significant growth in 2H'25 and into 2026.

What have we done to navigate tariffs

  *   Negotiated a 37% discount on product coming from China, giving us enough inventory to fully supply all new distribution coming on board for 2025 Back-to-School
  *   Secured space in a Bonded Warehouse, which will let us delay and potentially lower tariff payments
  *   Signed 3-year manufacturing partnership with an established Manufacturer based in Canada (start-up in August)
     *   We closely tracked the tariff situation with Canada, and while there remains some uncertainty, our products are USMCA compliant, so currently have no import tariffs
        *   The contract also has protections if tariffs are placed on our products in the future
     *   FruitBlox will be above a 40% Gross Margin, with opportunity to improve after start-up
     *   North American supply chain & improved payment terms will improve our cash conversion cycle by ~75 days

Key business update: Fundraise
We have incredibly high growth aspirations over the coming 12 - 18 months, as we expand retailers, introduce new products, and bring new Creators/IP onto the brand

  *   Distribution: We are tripling our total points of distribution during the upcoming back-to-school r

*Lowlights*

Meta CPAs rose ~50% over the past year, making new customer acquisition
unsustainable given our unit economics. We responded by cutting paid spend
50% and restructuring our campaigns which has negatively impacted top line
revenue.

our revenue. It carries better unit economics and a customer base we don’t
need to reacquire which is a stronger foundation to build on.

On track for mid-April production and June 2026 launch. Nutritional testing

   under-reporting incremental Shopify orders from in platform results by 24%.

*Lowlights*

   67% higher than our breakeven target. Given our profitability focus, we’re
   de-prioritizing this channel.

*Company Updates*



By mid-May, the Trump administration announced a pause on retaliatory
tariffs with China, effectively lowering our net tariff rates for
China-sourced products to 41%–63%. While still significantly higher than
the 10% tariff levels during the golden era of 2024, this provides us with
some breathing room. We're using this 90-day tariff pause to rebuild
inventory of China-sourced products (e.g., cup ramen), which will help
ensure business continuity at key retailers. It also gives us more time to
shift production out of China.



On the retail side, we plan to keep cups in rotation for any existing
retailers that already carry cups. We were able to negotiate a price
decrease with our China CoMan to help offset a portion of tariffs which
will allow us a bit of margi

*Monthly Revenue:* $1,566,913 *(-5.71% MoM)*

*Company Updates*



This month, volatility created opportunity and we moved fast to capitalize.



Following the 145% tariff hikes, we secured a 3,500-door acceptance at CVS
Health launching in September by quickly offering to fill vacated space
with 2 packet SKUs.



In parallel, we’ve proactively managed the tariff disruption on our
products. While our packet SKUs transitioned to Malaysia earlier this year,
our cup and toppings SKUs are still produced in China. Cups comprise ~8% of
our overall revenue but ~30% of our fast-growing wholesale business.



New tariff rates make cups unprofitable so we’ve made the decision to
cancel a July launch into ~1,800 new Albertsons/Safeway doors with 2 cup
SKUs to avoid bleeding cash if tariffs don’t adjust. We’re pushing them to
evaluate swapping to 2 packet SKUs instead for their next reset in October.



Our manufacturing partner Mike has proven to be an incredibly supportive
and value add partner. They accelerated R&D to transition toppings
production to Malaysia and introduced us to new paper cup co-man leads in
Thailand and Singapore. Our R&D team flew out to begin in-person
development conversations. We’re targeting early Q1 2026 for
comme

updates.subject: Endorphins – May 2025 Investor Update
summary: Investor update from Endorphins – May 2025
updates.rawText:
You are receiving this email because you are an investor in Endorphins or
one of our closest partners. Please consider everything below privileged
and confidential (e.g. don’t share with others without conferring with our
team first!) If you have anyone on your team that you would like to be
added to future updates, do not hesitate to reach out!


Hello team,


I would be remiss to start off this update without reiterating how grateful
I – and the broader Endorphins team – are to call you partners in what we
are building. Thank you for your ongoing support, we are thrilled to pursue
a generational company at the intersection of performance and community
alongside you.


I’ve had the chance to bring many of you up-to-speed on our progress, but
moving forward, we’ll be sharing investor updates every 4 to 6 weeks. This
is our first of many, so as always, I invite your feedback on the format
and the content.


In the meantime, I’m thrilled to be writing to you on the heels of some
tremendous momentum, more on that below.


Executive summary

   -

   Asks: we are looking for 1) channel partnerships to strategics in health
   and wellness 2) a full-time design hire with deep experience in consumer
   tech, 3) a glimpse into your VC firm rolodex.
   -

   Fundraise: we closed a $1M pre-seed in December 2024 led by Slow
   Ventures, with participation from Bungalow, Rebel Capital, and a slew of
   strategic angels.
   -

   Hiring: we brought on 2 founding engineers with a collective 25 years of
   experience, and who built engineering chops at the likes of Google,
   Palantir, and Capital One.
   -

   Product: we graduated from a 3rd party product to a 1st party
   mobile-first product with native apps on iOS and Android; we’ve logged
   7,500 app installs in the first 12 weeks.
   -

   Partnerships: we entered year two of our $440,000 partnership with New
   Balance. Additionally, we kicked off a $120,000 partnership

updates.rawText:
Hi all,



Hope everyone had wonderful holidays! I’m excited to share our year-end
investor update.



*2025 Metrics:*

·         *Revenue:* Increased 3.0x in 2025 to $112K of MRR

·         *Supported Integrations:* Increased 2.1x in 2025 to 120 systems

·         *Connection Count: *Increased 6.8x in 2025 to 1,672 connections



*Revenue Update:*
[image: image.png]

·         As of 12/31/25, we’re at $112K of contracted MRR ($1.34M of ARR)
across 54 customers.

·         In October and November, we added net +$6K of MRR ($68K of ARR):

o   +$11K from new customer wins

o   +$12K from existing customers increasing connections

o   Offset by -$17K from customer churn

·         November was our largest gross revenue month to-date, with +$15K
of new MRR, though much of it was offset by churn.



Churn drivers during this period:

·         One of our largest customers (~$9K of MRR) went through a
reorganization in November and shut down most divisions including the
inventory management product using Trackstar. While the winddown is still
ongoing and they continue to pay us, we removed this revenue from ARR.

·         In November and December, we proactively canceled contracts for
customers who signed earlier in the year but never went live on our API.



*Churn Commentary: *Once customers go live and meaningfully use Trackstar,
churn is close to zero. We dug into the data and saw the following:

·         70% of churned customers had 0-1 connections.

·         20% had 2-4 connections.

·         With two exceptions, every customer churned to-date had fewer
than 5 connections.

o   One exception was the customer referenced above who went through a
reorganization (100+ connections).

o   The other exception (with ~20 connections) was acquired, with the
acquiring entity becoming a customer.



This reinforces what we’ve said before: as an infrastructure product,
Trackstar is extremely sticky once cus

updates.rawText:
Hi all



Hope everyone has a great Thanksgiving! I’m excited to share our investor
update for September and October 2025.



*Revenue Update:*
[image: image.png]

·         As of 10/31/25, we’re at $106K of contracted MRR ($1.28M of ARR)
across 52 customers.

·         In September and October, we added net +$10K of MRR ($122K of
ARR):

o   +$5K from new customer wins

o   +$8K from existing customers increasing connections

o   Offset by -$3K from customer churn

·         In October, we started proactively canceling contracts for
customers who signed months ago but never went live. Most of the churn came
from this cleanup. This will continue in November and December as we remove
inactive accounts and strengthen the revenue base.


*Burn Update:*

·         *Cash Balance: *$1.94M at the end of October.

·         *Runway: *Burn in September and October averaged ~$85K, resulting
in ~23 months of runway.

o   We added two new hires this fall, and those costs are now fully
reflected in our burn.

o   Note on cash flow: while monthly invoicing has increased meaningfully,
there’s typically a ~2-month working capital lag before invoices convert to
cash flow.



*General Update:*

·         We feel great about the business. 2025 can be summarized by us
proving that the business not only works but works well. Customers love the
product, we’re successfully onboarding larger companies, and customers are
proactively pushing us to expand into new verticals. Our focus now is on
scalability and accelerating growth.

·         *Conferences: *We attended Parcel Forum, Pitney Bowes User
Conference, and Money20/20.

o   Money20/20 was our first fintech conference, and we’re leaning heavily
into this vertical. Lending companies can materially improve underwriting
and fraud prevention using WMS data. We’re seeing strong traction and
expect additional fintech wins heading into the year-end.

·         *Proof-of-Concepts:* H

updates.rawText:
Hi all,



I’m excited to share our investor update for July and August 2025.



*Revenue Update:*
[image: image.png]

·         As of 8/31/25, we’re at $96K of contracted MRR ($1.15M of ARR)
across 51 customers.

o   In July and August, we added a total of $18K of MRR ($211K of ARR).

o   Growth was driven by +$14K of MRR from new customer wins, +$5K of MRR
from existing customers increasing connections, offset by -$1K of MRR from
two early-stage software co.’s no longer using Trackstar.


*Burn Update:*

·         Cash balance at the end of August was $2.11M. The cash balance
reflects the $1.36M of new capital that we opportunistically received from
TMV and Y Combinator.

·         Burn in July and August averaged $67K, resulting in ~32 months of
runway at the August month-end cash balance.



*General Update:*

·         *Hiring: *We hired 2 new employees – adding a Solutions Engineer
and Backend Engineer to the team. The Solutions Engineer started in July,
and the Backend Engineer started in September.

·         *Conferences:* We attended the Cin7 Summit in August. Cin7 is a
private equity-owned inventory management system that we recently closed as
a customer. It is a large customer win.

o   Trackstar was featured throughout the Cin7 Summit as our API is a major
unlock in how Cin7 onboards customers.

·         *Content:* Same update as before. Content continues to be a solid
driver of brand awareness and inbound.



*Customer Update:*

·         *Customer Profile: *Same update as before. We continue to kick
off POCs and close larger software companies as customers. Specifically, we
closed Flexport in July and Cin7 in August. Both customers should become
large six-figure contracts as they add connections.



*Upcoming Initiatives:*

·         *Pipeline: *Our current pipeline is largely driven by
conferences, referrals, and content. We are kicking off an active outbound
push via automated flows.

updates.rawText:
Hi all,



I’m excited to share our investor update for May and June 2025.


*New Capital: *

·         We took $1.36M of new capital from existing investors – TMV and Y
Combinator – at a $25.0M post-money valuation. The capital was taken
opportunistically. It will be used to hire more quickly, while also
allowing us to hit higher ARR targets ahead of the Series A.



*How You Can Help: *

·         *Hiring – Backend Engineer: *We are looking for a Backend
Engineer with 3 – 5 years of experience. We’d love intros to any engineers
in your networks that you think highly of.


*Revenue Update:*
[image: image.png]

·         We’ve 2.1X’ed contracted revenue from the beginning of 2025
through the end of June 2025.

·         As of 6/30/25, we’re at $78.6K of contracted MRR ($943K of ARR)
across 47 customers. Trackstar powered 1000+ WMS connections across our
customers and proof-of-concepts in June.

o   In May and June, we added a total of $15.2K of MRR ($183K of ARR).
Growth was driven by 1) $11.0K of MRR from new customer wins, 2) $4.7K of
MRR from existing customers increasing connections, offset by 3) -$0.5K of
MRR from one early-stage software co. shifting in their business away from
functionality that relies on WMS data.



*Burn Update:*

·         Cash balance at the end of June was $992K, which included $111K
of new capital wired in June. $600K was wired in July, and the remaining
$650K of new capital will hit the balance sheet in early August. Cash on
the balance sheet, pro forma for the entirety of the new capital, is
$2.24M.

·         Burn in May and June averaged $68K, resulting in 15 months of
runway at the June month-end cash balance.

o   Pro forma for the $1.36M of new capital, runway is 33 months.

o   New revenue coming online within the next two to three months will
fully offset the incremental cost of upcoming hires.



*General Update:*

updates.rawText:
Happy new year!

Hope everyone had a great holidays and is rocking in the new year. We're
off to a good start this year. Our pace of development has increased
dramatically. We are officially cooking with our dev team becoming
incredibly fast by adopting AI tooling at a high level.

The pace of engineering has increased so dramatically that we've had to
reorient our product process. I spoke about it on a podcast recently here

multi-channel army of agents for small businesses - voice, text, chat,

*Challenges:*

updates.rawText:
Hi all,

Hope everyone had a great Thanksgiving and is geared up for a great holiday
break. Our steady growth continues. We're focused now on increasing the
rate of growth and pushing toward profitability.

*Highlights:*

   - *ARR: $1.5m - *up from $1.38m in November
   - MRR: $125k - up from $115k in November
   - Active customers: 1373 - up from 1252 in November
   - Accounts created over last 30 days: 601 - down from 609 in October
   - Usage over last 30 days: 213k minutes - down from 239k minutes in
   October
   - Conversion rate of trial to paid is now up to 15%-18%
   - Burn is improving - $170k last month and expected to be $140k this
   month - we are on the march toward sustainability

*MRR Graph:*
[image: image.png]


*Signups Graph:*
[image: image.png]

*Key wins:*

   - *Trial to paid conversion rate continues to improve, hitting 18% over
   the last few weeks*
   - Completely off VAPI and 100% on Bland now, bringing infra costs way
   down
   - First scheduling integrations are live - Google Calendar and Calendly
      - Will provide is with more value to spread across plans and
      distribution potential with partners
   - SMS website widget feature in the works to turn Rosie from uni-channel
   to multi-channel
      - Significant revenue potential from existing customer base
      - Points toward Rosie's evolution into a multi-channel sales/support
      agent for SMBs
   - ARPU currently $91/mo

*Challenges:*

   - While adding an average of around $10k in MRR per month is definitely
   not bad, we need to break through the linear growth and into our bigger
   potential
   - SEO has just not worked well at all - we are pulling back our
   investments here until our cash/burn position is better
   - Cold email didn't provide good enough ROI so we cut it

*GTM:*

   - New ad agency started this week
   - First integration partnerships in the works - Clickfunnels, Appointlet
   - both set to go

*It's been a challenging few weeks but we are battling through and making
progress. *We needed to slow down a bit to speed back up. We dialed back on
ad spend, made the Rosie 2.0 changes we had planned, worked with Bland to
improve our agent - and now we're ready to speed back up.

*Traction:*

      significant source of signups - our aim is to get a Rosie page on their
      site and also to ensure Rosie has a presence inside of their admin
      - Their team has been good to work with, though the transition from
      Vapi to Bland has been difficult at times. We've turned a corner with the
      help of their team and are now set to bring over the bulk of our
      userbase (which will have the added benefit of reducing expenses
by several
      thousand per month)

      that Rosie is a top priority for them
      - Once new prompt is updated next week we'll get an announcement out
   - New onboarding is live - much faster and more intuitive, and biggest
   strategic change is we now require a credit card on file to accept live
   calls (users can identify up to 3 numbers for testing that don't require a

updates.rawText:
Hello all!

*We hit $1m ARR, surpassed 1000 paying customers, and closed a $100k deal!
*Awesome, but we have work to do!

The good:

   - Breaking $1m ARR is great for burn and morale - we got there in 9
   months from the first paying customer. Awesome :)
   - We closed the big Valvoline deal for 200+ locations - they signed a
   3-year contract currently worth $100k+ per year (it will grow as they add
   more locations)
   - Team is doing great - the trip to SF together was incredible

The challenging:

   - Our growth has slowed down over the last few weeks - it's a
   combination of things impacting growth - switch to Bland and finding our
   footing on the agent, advertising challenges around targeting
   - We are rebuilding our agent quality back up on the Bland infrastructure
   - Refocusing our ad targeting and campaigns to bring in better-fit
   signups


*What we're doing about it, in this order:*

   - Improving acquisition elements of the funnel:
      - New homepage with updated positioning and copywriting - timeline:
      end of June
      - New account creation flow - timeline: live now and has had
      positive impact on homepage to signup conversion rate
      - New onboarding - timeline: end of June
   - Improving agent quality
      - Working with Bland on their new LLM
      - Improving our prompting and pathways with lots of feedback from
      customers
   - New features:
      - New voices, direct appointment scheduling, live transfers,
      integrations, outbound calls, etc are all up for consideration
   - Pricing and plans update

*Traction:*

   - *ARR: $1.08m - *up from $883k in May
   - MRR: $90k - up from $73k in May

updates.rawText:
Hello all!

We're approaching $1m ARR and are elevating our game on multiple fronts. We
find ourselves in an opportune moment where we can tackle several upgrade
projects that we're calling "Rosie 2.0"

The moment came about because we just completed a big project in which we
switched infrastructure providers. Rosie was originally built on Vapi
<https://vapi.ai/>. Their platform provided a great way to get to market
quickly with a solid product. But over the last few months, they've caused
us headaches and our confidence in them deteriorated.

In our search for a replacement, we found Bland <https://www.bland.ai/>.
Over the last few weeks, we've rebuilt Rosie on Bland's platform. We're
currently in a bit of purgatory with new signups going onto Bland, but
existing users are on Vapi. The full transition should be complete within a
few weeks and then we'll be able to move forward with gusto.

*Rosie 2.0 projects in progress:*

   - New homepage with updated positioning and copywriting
   - New onboarding flow, including lifecycle emails and notifications
   - Updated roadmap combining customer feedback with Bland's prebuilt
   functionality:
      - New voices, direct appointment scheduling, live transfers,
      integrations, outbound calls, etc are all up for consideration
   - Pricing and plans update

*Traction:*

   - *ARR: $883k - *up from $696k in April
   - MRR: $73k - up from $58k in April
   - Active customers: 947 - up from 763 in April
   - Accounts created over last 30 days: 1897 - up from 1887 in April
   - Usage over last 30 days: 95k minutes - up from 58k minutes in April

*MRR graph:*


*Signups graph:*


*Key wins:*

   - Jess conducted a traditional Sean Ellis PMF survey (we got 82
   responses) and the results were fantastic:
      - A full 50% of users said they'd be "Very Disappointed" if they
      couldn't use Rosie
         - *For context: "Strong PMF" is benchmarked at 40% of users being

updates.rawText:
Investors, Advisors & Friends,

We hope you are all well. Please find an update on the performance of
Adgile over the first quarter of 2025 below:

*Financial*

   - In Q1 ’25 Adgile recognized:
      - $1.3m in revenue (up from $871k in Q1 ‘24, or +49% YoY)
         - The highest quarterly revenue in company history!
      - $73k in net income (up from -$61k in Q1 ‘24, or +$135k YoY)
         - The highest quarterly net income in company history!
         - This also marks profitability over the trailing 12 months!
      - 42.5% gross margin (down from 47.5%, or -5% YoY)

This reduction in gross margin was driven by increased COGS due to rush
campaigns for several large clients to start the year. Additionally, we
granted larger than normal discounts to a few large clients, hoping they
will be prominent customers for years to come. We are working to get gross
margin closer to the 50% benchmark in future quarters.

While we expected to recognize over $100k in net income at the beginning of
the quarter, this reduction in gross margin, combined with increased SG&A
expenses (Employee bonus payments, increased performance metrics expenses)
resulted in the company missing that mark.

   - We have $3.1m in signed contracts that we expect to recognize in 2025
   - Current cash position:
      - $639k in cash
      - $318k in receivables
         - We expect to write off about $45k in bad debt this year related
         to campaigns where the client has since gone bankrupt. We are using a
         collections agency to recoup as much of this debt as
possible, but do not
         expect to recover it all.




Thrilled to see these trendlines continue in the right direction!



*Advertisers*

   - We had 46 new executions go live in Q1. Please find photos of some of
   our favorites here
   <https://c-pBh04.na1.hs-sales-engage.com/Ctc/UA+23284/c-pBh04/JkM2-6qcW6N1vHY6lZ3mbN2pzTxD6y0n4W6JHKDQ1T9ZHSW15R42x1gK0T1W4K9vB_51ld5WV9VkHd1B

updates.rawText:
Impatient Ventures Investors,

We're reaching out to share an opportunity to co-invest in Oats Overnight.
<https://www.oatsovernight.com/> As you may know, Oats Overnight was
Impatient Ventures' first investment and the company and team have
continued to exceed our expectations. Oats is on an impressive growth
trajectory, currently operating at a $240M revenue run rate with 287K
monthly subscribers. The company grew 71% in Q1'25 compared to Q1'24 and is
profitable. Oats Overnight is also rapidly expanding its retail
footprint, available
in over 12,000 stores across Costco, Walmart, Target, Sam’s Club, Sprouts
and more.

Costco Update in the last 48 hours from the Oats Overnight team

Oats has secured a $20M national buy commitment from Costco for the back
half of 2025, reinforcing its position as a long-term staple at Costco.
Thanks to its placement in Department 20, one of Costco’s only centrally
managed buying divisions, Oats launched nationally from day one—bypassing
the typical regional structure. After converting a $12M test order into
“everyday item” status in just two weeks, the brand entered Costco’s
auto-replenishment system and is now generating ~$1M in weekly sales as a
top-performing SKU within the Costco system. Today, Oats is meeting in
person with Costco’s national buyer to align on 2026 plans and present
exclusive innovation, signaling continued deep partnership and growth ahead.

ASK: If you'd like to participate, please complete your documents and fund
your investment by 5/30 via this AngelList Deal Room:
https://portal.angellist.com/l/dr/fb3b895c

We believe Oats Overnight is uniquely positioned to lead the next
generation of functional nutrition with high-frequency, craveable protein
products and a best-in-class business model. Please see our memo at the
bottom of this email for additional details about the company and why we
still see an attractive investment opportunity. A deck is available in the
deal room li

updates.rawText:
Dear Investors,

We hope all is well and that 2026 is off to a great start.

2025 was a transformative year for Spritz Society. We evolved from a
primarily direct-to-consumer brand with limited retail presence into a
nationally distributed brand, now available in over 5,000 points of
distribution across more than 40 states, including approximately 1,400
Target, Harris Teeter and Jewel Osco stores.

*Distribution Partnership Transition*

In late 2024, we signed a sales and distribution agreement with E & J Gallo
to help us scale efficiently through their route-to-market. That
partnership was instrumental in enabling our national Target launch and
accelerating our growth. We would not have been able to bootstrap national
distribution without their support.

As 2025 progressed, headwinds in the beverage alcohol industry led Gallo to
concentrate resources on its largest brands, limiting incremental growth
for Spritz Society outside of Target. After thoughtful discussion, we
mutually agreed that the partnership was no longer the right long-term fit.
Gallo worked with us to exit the agreement without any switching costs and
committed to supporting a seamless transition.

We are excited to share that we have found our new strategic partner in
Precept Wine & Spirits. After evaluating companies across the beverage
landscape, Precept emerged as the clear choice. Precept brings dedicated
sales and marketing resources, national infrastructure through Southern
Glazer’s Wine & Spirits (the largest beverage alcohol distributor in the
country), and a shared commitment to building a category winner. When we
previously worked with SGWS directly as an independent supplier in NY and
CA, we lacked the internal advocacy needed to compete for attention within
their portfolio. As a Precept brand, we now have it. With this partnership
as our foundation, we are targeting aggressive retail and velocity growth
over the next 24 to 36 months.

Additionally, t

*Strictly Confidential - For Investors of Spritz Society Only*

Dear Investors,

We hope this note finds you well. As we head into the RTD industry’s busy
summer season, we’re excited to share a major update on Spritz Society’s
progress through the first part of 2025 -- a truly transformational time
for the brand.


   - *National Launch*: In partnership with Gallo, Spritz Society rolled
   out nationally across 40+ states, supported by 60 distributors and is now
   available in 3000+ retail accounts.
   - *Revenue*: We generated $922k in Q1 2025 revenue (vs. a projected
   $670k) and sold more than 25k cases.
   - *Burn*: We continue to reduce burn, with monthly net operating losses
   averaging below $100k during Q1 2025. While fluctuations will occur based
   on marketing investments, we remain focused on sustainable growth.
   - *Marketing*: In addition to our organic marketing efforts, we launched
   a comprehensive national marketing and communications strategy featuring
   in-store tastings, local activations, paid media, content production, and
   event sponsorships. Highlights can be found in the attached deck.
   - *Retail Velocity*: Our marketing efforts are driving significant store
   traffic, rate-of-sale, and repeat performance. Target sales increased 200%
   WoW post-launch. Pink Lemonade is performing at ~$15/week/store, with the
   portfolio averaging ~$12/week/store -- well above Target’s benchmark of
   $9/SKU/week/store for established brands. Over 20k four-packs were sold
   through the register in our first month.
   - *Margin Improvement*: Q1 gross margin improved to 53%, a major leap
   from 30% in 2024, reflecting improvements in our supply chain and product
   format.
   - *Direct-to-Consumer Transition*: We formally exited our self-managed
   DTC business, previously weighed down by 3PL warehousing, shipping, and tax
   costs. As a national brand, we’re now enabling online sales through
   physical alcoho

updates.rawText:
Halfdays investors + advisors!

Hope you're doing well. I wanted to share a quick update with you all. We've had a really strong year — lots of growth and exciting momentum — but the recent tariff developments are putting unexpected pressure on the business.

As a result, we’re planning to raise a bit more equity capital to navigate this moment and stay on track. I’ve attached a short memo with more details.

Halfdays is raising a $4M Series A bridge round following strong FY25 performance, including $13.7M in net revenue (up 50% YoY), 4x wholesale growth, and the launch of successful retail and brand initiatives. The company forecasts $20M+ in FY26 revenue and its first profitable year, supported by major wholesale orders and high-profile collaborations. The raise is primarily to cover increased upfront costs due to new tariffs, with funds allocated to inventory, shipping, and continued product and marketing expansion.

*Please contact myself and Liz Anthony (on cc) if you're interested in participating no later than* *May 16* *.* *
*

Best,

Ariana

updates.rawText:
Hi ,



As shared in our last update, 2025 has been a transformative year for
Sanzo, evolving from a beloved Whole Foods–centered brand into one ready to
scale nationally. Over this past quarter, we’ve experienced some growing
pains and learned valuable lessons as we pursue our long-term brand
strategy.
*Why This Evolution Matters*

As a reminder, Whole Foods Market represents ~1% of total U.S. grocery
sales, while “conventional” grocery (e.g. Kroger, Albertsons) and
“mass/club” retailers (e.g. Walmart, Target, Costco, Sam’s Club) account
for over 90%.



To grow sustainably, both in revenue and profitability, Sanzo must succeed
in this 90%+. Simply put, winning at Whole Foods or in alternative channels
like Foodservice alone isn’t a viable long-term model for a beverage brand.
*Brand & Product Evolution*

As we began to grow the brand beyond Whole Foods in 2024, we identified a
need to evolve the brand in 3 key ways, all of which have now been
completed:

   1. *More flavor:* Informed by consumer feedback from our Costco roadshow
   last fall.
   2. *Operational efficiency:* Rebuilding our supply chain to be more
   cost-effective, resilient, and scalable while maintaining clean labels,
   “better-for-you” nutritionals.
   3. *Improved price competitiveness:* Lowering price to better compete
   with category leaders and win new distribution beyond Whole Foods-like
   retailers, and improving gross margins in the process.

*Distribution & Performance Update*

*Conventional / Mass / Club (>90% of the market, we currently have <1%
household penetration)*

   1. Momentum: at the same time that we’ve added distribution, we’ve also
   increased our sales velocities, which sets us up well for next year.
   2. Our success here is leading to the following in late 2025 / early
   2026:
      1. *Walmart:* 1,200-1,500 store expansion (April 2026)
      2. *Costco:* LA rotation *finally* launching (December 2025)
      3. *Target:

updates.rawText:
Hi Nik,



This past quarter may be the most consequential in Sanzo’s six-year
history.



*The headline:* our updated formulations + pricing strategy are hitting
benchmarks at Walmart, and *last week Walmart soft authorized the brand to
scale from 130 stores to 1,200+ in April 2026. *



This is the first and clearest validation of the painstaking strategy we
undertook over the past 18 months to evolve Sanzo from a beloved Whole
Foods brand into one ready to scale nationally and truly live out our
mission of bridging cultures.



For context, “natural” grocery retailers like Whole Foods and Sprouts
Farmers Market represent ~6–7% of total U.S. grocery sales, while
“conventional” grocery (e.g. Kroger, Albertsons) and “mass/club” retailers
(e.g. Walmart, Target, Costco, Sam’s Club) account for >90%.



As part of our evolution, we’ve now:

   1. Rebuilt our supply chain to deliver more flavor, more efficiency, and
   more resiliency, while retaining a clean label and ‘better-for-you’ calorie
   and sugar counts.
   2. Reduced 6-pack pricing at key retail partners. Highlights:
      1. Whole Foods Market: *$7.99* on 8/1
      2. Walmart: *$6.98* on 7/1
   3. Modernized our operating expense infrastructure to become more
   capital efficient (see below).
   4. Secured a new West Coast manufacturer (also produces Poppi, OLIPOP,
   Liquid Death) to meaningfully lower freight expenses as we scale (launching
   November 2025)
   5. Greenlit packaging updates to create a cleaner look that enhances
   readability and shelf impact (see attached)

With our upcoming Walmart expansion, Costco rotations on deck, and interest
building across other major national retailers, Sanzo is now
well-positioned to compete at the next level.



Thank you, as always, for your continued support.



Best,
Sandro

   - ~70% growth and profitability on deck
   - First Costco LA rotation (Dec ’25 throug

We just wrapped up our 1Q25 Board of Directors meeting, and I’m thrilled to
share some exciting updates on Sanzo’s growth, financial performance.
*🚀 Record-High Revenue, Profitability*

We are on-track to achieve break-even / profitability in 12-15 months.

recipe updates:

and foodservice doors *and with plans to launch our first 2 Costco
rotations in the LA and Midwest regions in 2H25.

April. While early, this flavor has already become a top 3 flavor for the
brand, and in ~15% of stores mandarin shot to #1 within the first 2 weeks.
We are working aggressively to win new distribution for 2H25.

✅ *Updated Supply Chain / Formulations*: Our updated formulations hit
Walmart shelves in April and will hit shelves across rest-of-market in
June/July.



Initial reception has been strong as we've maintained our nutritional
macros and compliance with Whole Foods Market's Food Ingredient Quality

These updates simplify our operations, reduce our costs and environmental

Hi everyone,

Brands like Virgin Voyages, Nespresso, Paramount+, eBay, Puma, Madewell, and Mercedes-Benz helped drive a record Q1, delivering the highest quarterly EBITDA in #paid history at $1.3 million—a $1.5 million beat against our budgeted ($0.2 million) loss, and a $1.4 million swing from a ($0.1 million) loss in Q1 2025.

On the topline, we missed Gross Revenue at 84% to budget due to a slower January, though we focused on higher-margin revenue. The trade-off was favorable, as Net Revenue hit 121% to budget at $7 million, up 32% YoY, and Gross Margin expanded from 48% to 54%, leading to a record Operating Profit of over $1.1 million.

All figures $USD

*Q1 2026* (vs Q1 2025)

Gross Revenue increased 14% to $11.1 million (84% to Budget)

Net Revenue increased 32% to $7 million (121% to Budget)

OPEX increased 6% to $4.8 million (86% to Budget)

1,029 creators were paid $3.2 million

Brand NPS decreased 6% to 67

Contribution Margin increased from 23% to 34%

*Cash*

$6.5 million cash in bank as at end of March 2026, forecasting to end FY2026 at $6.8 million.

*1,000 c reators on Virgin Voyages*

We made history putting 1,000 creators on a Virgin Voyages ship ( press release ( https://www.virginvoyages.com/press/latest-releases/largest-tiktok-creator-influencer-cruise-ship-takeover ) ). Pulling this off required significant upgrades to our platform, including a new foundational AI layer, Atlas, enabling advanced analytics and performance data.

*Atlas + AI ops*

Atlas is our creator enrichment product, which forms the foundation for our suite of AI tools like Event Analytics, Predictive Lift, and Brand Safety. AI continues to transform our internal operations. Engineering throughput increased 113% QoQ and velocity increased 146% QoQ.

*Pinterest p artnership*

We have officially partnered with Pinterest, kicking off campaigns with Apple, Loblaw, and Scotiabank ( press release ( https://finance.yahoo.com/news/paid-pinterest-an

Hi Everyone,

#paid has maintained growth and profitability throughout H1 2025.

Brands like Meta, Cadillac, Snickers, Urban Outfitters, and Roots helped drive Q2 revenue of $8.7M, down 9% q/q though contributing to $18.4 million for H1 2025, up 30% YoY.

While we fell short of our revenue goal for Q2, OPEX was $1.2 million under budget, leading to an operating profit of $0.2 million YTD vs budgeted loss of $0.1 million.

All figures in USD

*Q2 2025 (compared to Q2 2024)*

Gross revenue decreased 9% to $8.7 million (71% to Budget)

Net revenue decreased 17% to $4.5 million (71% to Budget)

OPEX decreased 19% to $4 million (78% to Budget)

609 creators earned $3.2 million

Brand NPS of 77

Contribution margin of 21% (vs 22% FY24)

*Cash*

We e nded the quarter with $1.9 million in cash and $9.2 million in receivables. Our biggest cash outflows are as a result of ramping up hiring and spending to support continued growth.

*Powering the Time 100 list*

We are proud to have powered the inaugural Time 100 creator list ( https://time.com/collections/time100-creators-2025/ ) !

*New Exec*

We are excited to welcome Katie Dombrowski ( https://www.linkedin.com/posts/katiedombrowski_at-google-i-bet-on-intent-at-pinterest-activity-7349295044291383297-FMPX? ) to the team as our Chief Commercial Officer. Katie's experience at Pinterest and Google and her operational rigor will be key to scaling our enterprise GTM during this next phase of growth.

*How you can help*

The #paid Creator Marketing Summit ( http://creatormarketingsummit.com/ ) is coming up this week on October 23rd in NYC. If you know any folks who are CMOs, VPs, or Directors of Marketing at large enterprise consumer brands, please let me know so I can see if we can get them an invite.

Thank you. Let me know if you have any thoughts or questions.

All the very best,

Bryan

Hi Everyone,

We've had a strong start to 2025.

Brands like IKEA, Roots, Ford, Revlon, and Paramount helped drive Q1 Enterprise Revenue up 81% to $9.4 million, compared to $5.2 million in the prior year. While we grew net revenue 171% compared to the prior year, and exceeded targets at 108% to Budget, we were under on OPEX, keeping costs flat compared to the prior year. This lead to $0.6 million in additional operating profit for the quarter vs budget, and over $2 million in free cash flow for the quarter.

All figures $USD

*Q1 2025 (compared to Q1 2024)*

Gross Revenue increased 67% to $9.7 million (109% to Budget)

Net Revenue increased 171% to $5.3 million (108% to Budget)

OPEX increased 2% to $4.6 million (96% to Budget)

465 creators earned $3.4 million

Brand NPS of 71

Contribution Margin of 23%, (vs 13% in Q1 2024)

*Cash*

We generated p ositive cash flow of $0.6 million/month in Q1, ending the quarter with $3.9 million in cash and $6.3 million in receivables. W e're now ramping up on hiring and spending to support continued growth.

*We shipped Snapchat and YouTube in Q1*

We shipped full end-to-end integrations with Snapchat ( press release ( https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/paid-announces-new-creator-discovery-api-and-expanded-partnership-with-snapchat-in-north-america-302362141.html#:~:text=NEW%20YORK%2C%20Jan.,community%20and%20top%20brand%20partners. ) ) and YouTube ( see announcement ( https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ashley-riske-62070229_paid-paid-activity-7328503722714550273-oq-P?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAnCVyYB6ulPqUkVLfrlgruCfuTS7uQqgUQ ) ), so creators can now monetize their audiences on additional channels, and brands can now tap into the power of creators across Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube on #paid.

*How you can help*

Open to recommendations around who would be a great fit for a Series C lead investor. Also, if there's anyone I should connect with at Cannes Lio

updates.subject: Q1 2026 Create Wellness Investor Update
summary: Net Revenue: $24.1M
Contribution Margin: $6.1M (25% margin)
EBITDA: $2.3M (9.6% margin)
updates.rawText:
Hi All,

Hope you are well. Here is the full Q1 2026 Create Wellness Investor Update
<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1oWJdu0dmRJ99PQXBlcPgGO_ZaMKZ8KPTsjTaet_8MwQ/edit?tab=t.0>
and
a summary below.

Create had a strong Q1 with $24.1M in net revenue, 55% gross margin, 25%
contribution margin, and 9.6% EBITDA margin. It was our biggest quarter to
date, and it felt like it. But what excites us even more is what Q1 has set
in motion for Q2.

This quarter we kicked off a full brand refresh across packaging, creative
assets, and tone of voice. The brand looks and feels much more mature both
online and on-shelf. We launched Creatine + Electrolytes, our biggest new
product launch ever, right at the tail end of the quarter. We prepared for
our nationwide April Target reset, which brings 5 new SKUs into the mix and
takes us to 8 SKUs across our full product line. We got our beloved gummies
ready for single-serve format. And we made some meaningful hires that we
are really proud of.

Q1 Results

   -
   - Net Revenue: $24.1M
   - Gross Margin: 55%
   - Contribution Margin: $6.1M (25% margin)
   - EBITDA: $2.3M (9.6% margin)

Q2 is about building the right foundation including team, processes, and
relationships, so we can scale what's working and launch what's next with
conviction. New flavors, new channels, new bets.

2026 is a big year for Create. We're doubling down on everything that makes
us the modern creatine brand, and we're seeing all of the work from 2025
start to compound.

Thank you for your continued support, as always.

- Sienna + Dan

updates.subject: Q3 2025 Create Wellness Investor Update
summary: Contribution Margin: $2.4M (+36% QoQ)
*EBITDA:* $1.0M (+30% QoQ)
updates.rawText:
Hi All,

Hope you are well. Here is the full Q3 2025 Create Wellness Investor Update
<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Uk1AX3uU0JG7iY3eL9NXDzvB-XJw5IPs_9AHs1rNs9o/edit?tab=t.0>
and
a summary below.

During the quarter, Create generated $16.5M in net revenue (+26% QoQ) and
$1.0M in EBITDA (+30% QoQ) while launching full-chain with Target, Sprouts,
and Vitamin Shoppe. Our ability to sustain high growth while maintaining
profitability underscores disciplined execution, efficient scaling, and
strong retail fundamentals. Early performance across all three new retail
partners has exceeded benchmarks, validating Create’s positioning as the
leading mass-market creatine brand.

We also made meaningful strides in building out the team and product
portfolio. The company grew from 6 to 15 full-time employees, strengthening
our operational core and marketing capabilities under an expanding
leadership team. On the product front, we launched Sour Peach, and
finalized formulation for a new product line and form factor that will
debut early next year.

Q3 Summary Metrics

   -

   Net Revenue: $16.5M (+26% QoQ)
   -

   Marketing Efficiency: 2.50x (flat QoQ)
   - Contribution Margin: $2.4M (+36% QoQ)
   - *EBITDA:* $1.0M (+30% QoQ)

Our conviction hasn’t changed. We’re more bullish than ever on Create’s
trajectory. We’re operating as a truly omnichannel business, building a
brand that performs across DTC, Amazon, and retail. The next phase is about
depth: expanding distribution, scaling awareness, and cementing Create as
the household name in daily creatine.

What would the world look like if everyone were 10% stronger? We’re
beginning to find out.

Thank you for your continued support!

- Sienna + Dan

Achieved 97% NRR with zero churns in March. Churn ARR down 22% QoQ.
6 new logo wins, including SteelSeries ($140K ARR) — our largest deal ever, closed via cold call

MMM & Incrementality Contractor / Consultant: Our omni ads capability is a huge opportunity & tangential to the MMM space. We want to get deeper on positioning our value to brands & the ad platforms (Meta, Google, TikTok)

Pricing and packaging: A Q2 priority is adjusting our pricing - likely a com. If you know operators or advisors who have navigated a pricing model shift at a similar-stage SaaS company, we'd genuinely value the conversation.
A+ SDR referrals: We have one SDR ramping and are actively looking to add a second. Outbound-first, curious, coachable.

Web migration help: We're moving to brij.ai (for a variety of reasons, including tracking issues with our app sharing the same domain as our marketing site) and looking for recommendations on web developers with experience in domain migrations and SEO-safe redirects.

AI Use Cases at Scaling Startups: There is no doubt our use of AI is making us 10x more efficient. We're always looking for new use cases for inspiration - share the best things you're seeing from portcos - from automating internal processes to generating ROI calculators

updates.rawText:
Hi everyone,

As we enter 2026, I want to start with a simple takeaway: after navigating
one of the most volatile trade environments we’ve ever seen, Portless is
now de-risked, well-built, and positioned to aggressively scale. We are
incredibly excited about the year ahead.

2025 was not easy. Between the trade war, removal of de minimis, and the
need to rebuild large parts of our shipping, product, and billing
infrastructure, we were forced to operate in constant motion. Despite that,
we grew revenue 82% year-over-year, from $17.8M to approximately $32.5M,
while building a much stronger foundation for the business.

*State of the business*

Over the past year, we deliberately focused on strengthening the core:

   - Built out a strong leadership team across Sales, Marketing, Services,
   RevOps, and Partnerships
   - Opened our Vietnam FC and streamlined bonded China operations
   - Completed the core Portless tech stack
   - Graduated the Portless Direct Injection, our end-to-end fulfillment
   solution out of beta, making it a meaningful competitive advantage
   - Launched our first suite of Portless Services, advancing our “factory
   floor to customer front door” vision

There is still operational complexity ahead (HS codes, EU de minimis,
adding a US partner FC), but the difference now is that we have the team
and infrastructure to solve these proactively. With the core business
de-risked, 2026 is the year we shift fully into growth mode.

*Q4 achievements *

Q4 was a strong close to the year:

   - Smooth peak-season execution
   - Positive EBITDA due to strong gross margins despite carrier
   surcharges, reinforcing pricing power and unit economics
   - Rolled out three services (Portless Capital, Package Protection, and
   Inventory Protection)
   - Signed a few high-quality, multi-million dollar deals
   - Stabilized A/R collection and tariff billing
   - Closed our venture debt facility

*GTM focus and 2026 outl

updates.rawText:
Hi everyone,

I will begin with one high-level thought: now that we are through the
tariff war and have only increased revenue and our sales trajectory, it is
crystal clear that this opportunity is unbelievably massive and growing by
the day. Yes, there are challenges and difficulties, but that is to be
expected and even embraced. I feel incredibly fortunate to be able to run
this specific company at this specific moment in time. We are building
something truly special.

*Challenges:*

   - Unfortunately, we had to part ways with our CRO after just a few
   weeks; it was clear that, while he was a fantastic person, he was not the
   right fit for the role. Fortunately, after two months of recruiting and
   conducting interviews, we have found a replacement we are incredibly
   excited about. He is starting with us on Dec 8th.
   - The need to correctly determine and assign HTS codes to 150,000+
   constantly changing SKUs is incredibly complex and impacts every downstream
   operation – customs clearance, billing, etc. We are still in the process of
   getting it right.
   - We’ve also been feeling some pain managing billing across millions of
   shipments from three fulfillment centers, especially with all the tariff
   changes. The good news is our internal billing engine is going live soon,
   which should fully smooth that out.

*Opportunities:*

   - Tariffs have gone from a headwind to a tailwind:
      - “Tariff deferment” is now a core part of our offering
      - The ability to ship in a compliant manner with reduced risk of CBP
      holds is winning us new, larger brands.

      - Operational complexity is a strategic advantage:
      - Both China and the US have introduced additional data requirements.
      Some of them, like the requirement for exporters to list the
factories tax
      ID, will help us speed up many of our service offerings, which require
      tight integration with merchants’ factories.
      -

updates.rawText:
Hi Everyone.

Please see below for our Q2 update which has been one of the most
challenging, yet rewarding, quarters we have faced in the last few years.
We were fortunate to navigate it successfully and have never been better
positioned for the future.


*The ending of the de minimis exemption on May 2nd was challenging for
multiple reasons:*

   - First and foremost, it forced us to rebuild almost every single
   process we had created to date - from shipment method to customs clearance
   to our merchant portal to invoicing - and do it on the fly, while being
   bombarded daily with mixed messages from the White House and CBP about how
   tariffs would be rolled out.

   - Merchants and end consumers were spooked by the tariff wars, leading
   to a sharp downturn in both marketing and consumer spending.

   - Prospects went into “wait-and-see” mode as they waited to see if
   China, Vietnam, or a potential third country would be the ideal
   manufacturing location.


*Fortunately, these challenges turned out to be incredible opportunities:*

   - Rebuilding our processes forced us to experiment with direct injection
   (directly shipping packages ourselves via using carrier networks), which
   has shown incredible results - an average of <5 day delivery times with
   lower volatility and more consistent results. This will be a strategic
   game-changer for us.

   - Our investment in understanding how to import orders while being fully
   compliant with CBP guidelines has enabled us to boost our reputation as the
   market leader and win over large brands that don’t want to risk
   non-compliance. It also led to TV appearances on the BBC and Bloomberg, and
   print coverage in the WSJ, Bloomberg, CNBC, and others.

   - The (current) end state of tariffs - 30% on China and 20% (plus de
   minimis) on Vietnam - is a perfect level for us: high enough that brands
   are still looking to optimize their supply chain (and our ta

sources: pdf:Portless Raises $18M As End of De Minimis Drives Logistics Demand - Business Insider.pdf
updates.rawText:
Hi Everyone,

Today we announced our Series A funding round that we closed a few months
ago (note that the announced amount includes $8M of converting SAFEs in
2024 and the $10M in February for a total of a $18M Series A round).  The
aim of this announcement is to help cement us as the market leader in an
uncertain macroeconomic environment.

Please see here (non-paywall PDF attached):
https://www.businessinsider.com/portless-raises-vc-funding-de-minimis-end-drives-logistics-demand-2025-5)
and consider sharing it with your networks.

I also wanted to update you about how we are faring in the face of constant
tariff changes, including yesterday’s late-day court ruling striking down
the executive order-issued tariffs.

The simple answer is, we are set up to succeed no matter which direction
tariffs go:

   - At the peak 145% tariffs, we had merchants clamoring to use our
   Vietnam facility.
   - At the current 30% tariffs, our pipeline and lead inbound is the best
   it has ever been, as every company is suddenly assessing their supply
   chain; leveraging tariff deferment with our model is a great value prop
   that is winning us business. For context, brands that import $1M of goods
   into the US that don’t use our model need to pay an upfront $500k to the
   CBP before they sell a single item. Portless customers don’t pay any import
   duties when the goods are in our warehouse, they only pay import duties
   when the individual shipment crosses the border, after the brand has
   already collected the money from the customer. A massive cash flow
   advantage.
   - If yesterday’s court decision stands and the de minimis exemption
   resumes, we return to a world where shipping with Portless helps brands
   save significant sums on their tariffs, in addition to the primary benefits
   of cash flow and inventory agility.

Revenue: In our board projections for our ~$48M 2025 target, we had
projected $7.78M of revenue for January t

updates.rawText:
*CONFIDENTIAL: This is an update for our investors & advisors only - please
do not forward*
Dear Partners,

Hope everyone is having a great start to the summer!

*Q2 Recap*

We had a strong Q2 despite a turbulent macro-economic environment driving
$47.7MM in total revenue1. *This represents 41% YoY growth, and $14MM above
the same period last year, despite price increases going into effect
mid-quarter. *

Some big drivers of our Q2 sales performance were our *Mother’s Day*
<https://www.carawayhome.com/collections/mothers-day-picks> & *Father’s Day*
<https://www.carawayhome.com/celebrate-fathers-day> campaigns, as well as
the launch of our new Airtight Food Storage Containers
<https://www.carawayhome.com/products/glass-airtight-container-set>.

   - With our Mother’s Day & Father’s Day campaigns, we featured bundled
   products & tested partnered GWPs (gift with purchase) above a certain order
   value threshold. For Mother’s Day, we offered a Caraway designed & branded
   MATE the Label sweatshirt
   <https://www.carawayhome.com/products/caraway-x-mate-organic-home-cook-sweatshirt>,
   and for Father’s Day we offered a code for a free Butcher Box
   <https://www.butcherbox.com/> redemption. Both campaigns drove higher
   AOVs and sold out of the GWPs toward the end of the promotional period –
   this indicates that our customers are willing to spend more for a free item
   & shows that GWPs can play a key role as part of our promotional toolkit.
   - Our April launch of the Airtight Storage Set & Singles has been very
   successful, and we see potential for the Airtight program to grow into one
   of our key product lines. The Airtight Set was our #8 product in Net Sales
   for Q2, and the whole collection drove $1.45MM combined. Expanding the
   Caraway product portfolio continues to support retention and LTV growth,
   with 49% of Airtight orders coming from repeat customers.
   - Overall the eCom channels continued to maintain s

Bezel Investors,

focused heavily on operational efficiency to support the holiday rush. They
also struck key partnerships with American Express and Kalshi. Continuing
the momentum, 2026 is already starting strong with the lowest CACs
(customer acquisition costs) ever and GMV pacing meaningfully ahead of goal.

Hello Friends and Investors,

TL;DR: A few weeks ago, on the heels of our viral growth, we officially kicked off our seed round and have already closed $1.1M in fresh capital from notable angels and funds (including the founder of Venmo, early investors in Poppi, recently acquired for $2B, and world-class restaurant group owners). We’re in late-stage conversations with several lead funds and are targeting a $4-5M close by September. On the growth side, BiteSight has scaled from 7K beta users to 120K+ downloads with another 100K on our waitlist for new city expansion. Plus, we have now built a social following of over 100K followers across TikTok and Instagram, up from a few thousand in June. Product velocity is at an all-time high. The team has shipped a redesigned AI-powered search experience, a refreshed onboarding flow, updates to social features, and new updates to the discover page. These changes have led to us 3x’ing our activation rate (from 5% → 15%) and raising first-time reorder rates to 64%. Fundraising has been very time-consuming, but the team is keeping up the momentum.

summary: Our roadmap this quarter is incredibly important for advancing our current system and professionalizing Nostra as we move up market.*
SOC 2 security compliance
Updated Authentication so we can connect Nostra customers' data into Claude so they can analyze all of their server and browser data within Claude (Auth done this quarter, data is longer project)

Work displaying bot traffic data into Nostra's portal. We are seeing significant adoption of our bot protection tool despite close to no analytics. This will allow clients to analyze their bot traffic more directly rather than relying on other platforms.
