updates.rawText:
*Hi All,*

We closed our third straight quarter of growth (after 2 quarters in a row of contraction in H1 ’25), grew from $3.4M → $3.6m (MRR *12), reduced burn by ~20% and are on the precipice of tipping over into being a no-brainer solution (with the references to back us up) for brands doing >$20m a year. There is a lot to be excited about at Nostra. With that said, we definitely have a lot of work to do, especially on the implementation and data side of the business.  This quarter we will win / lose based on the quality of our implementations.

*Product*

*Our roadmap this quarter is incredibly important for advancing our current system and professionalizing Nostra as we move up market.*

• SOC 2 security compliance

• Updated Authentication so we can connect Nostra customers' data into Claude so they can analyze all of their server and browser data within Claude (Auth done this quarter, data is longer project)

• Data work to display Nostra's impact on different segments for our implementation team so that we can deploy Nostra more effectively (iOS, android, desktop, mobile etc)

• Work displaying bot traffic data into Nostra's portal. We are seeing significant adoption of our bot protection tool despite close to no analytics. This will allow clients to analyze their bot traffic more directly rather than relying on other platforms.

• A new version of our implementation system that would utilize LLMs (AI) to make work 80%+ more efficient. This project is multiple quarters of work (started in Q4 of last year).

*Highlights*

• Best Q1 retention / growth since we have been in market. We really only had 1 churn from an established brand. The rest were super small customers we don't target anymore.

• True ARR on annual contracts grew from $2.3 to $2.68M.

• Burn is lower than anticipated (~$225k vs projected $300k).

• Edge Protect, our bot protection product, is proving to be a strong second product. We are seeing retailers not able to ha

updates.subject: Nostra Q4 Investor Update
summary: First of all… Go Hoosiers!*
Customers* : 133 → 131
ARR (End of Quarter)* : $2.9M → $3.4M
Bank Balance* : $5.8M → $4.9M
Runway* : ~15 months at $300K/month burn - if/when we grow this number will shift
updates.rawText:
Hi all,

*First of all… Go Hoosiers!*

We’re closing out the year with signs of momentum. It hasn’t been a linear path, but the business feels stronger than it has in a long time across revenue quality, retention, close rates, culture, and infrastructure.

That said, we are focused on execution in H1. Historically, H1 has been a challenging period for us, but we believe this year will be different. Most of our customers are now on annual contracts, and our new pricing model, along with continued progress on the core product, has proven effective at upselling and renewing early with satisfied merchants.

Our focus for 2026 is to continue growing our mid-market Shopify business while also launching our enterprise motion, targeting brands with $250M+ in GMV on Shopify and $1B+ off-platform. If we can grow from $3.4M to $5.1M in ARR with a strong enterprise foundation, we will consider that a success.

If we maintain strong retention through H1 and continue executing, we expect burn to drop as revenue and margin increase. That would put us in a position to either raise a Series B ( exceeding plan ), organize a small financing to bridge to profitability (most likely plan) or explore strategic options. That said, we will need to see how H1 unfolds, ideally stacking four consecutive quarters of growth, before fully understanding our options.

Highlights

----------

* 

Closed our largest contract ever ($235K), validating our value in the upper mid-market and proving we can command premium pricing when positioned effectively

* 

ARR from annual contracts grew 109% from $1.1M to $2.3M in 2025

* 

Close rates (BANT to Closed Won) continue to improve, including  ~40 percent win rate for opportunities created in Q3

* 

Patent progress continues, we were advised that we will receive a patent on our original AI personalization system. This will be on top of our patent on the Edge Delivery Engine

* 

Mid-market wins are increasingly partner-l

updates.subject: Nostra Investor Update Q3 2025
summary: Investor update from Nostra
updates.rawText:
All,

After a long stretch of product work and tighter GTM spend, we’re starting to see early signs of momentum. NRR came in at 99% for Q3—our strongest retention to date. While we expect some known churn in October, it’s encouraging to see the product and pricing model beginning to hold. With that said, we remain "cautiously optimistic" as Q1 has historically been our roughest quarter. If we make it out of Q1 with strong retention we will know that we solved the problem.

Tactically, we’re continuing to focus most of our effort on mid-market Shopify accounts, while opportunistically leaning into enterprise and self-service SMB bets. On its own, our mid market Shopify business is profitable and generating cash flow to invest in R&D, enterprise and self service.

From a financial perspective, our burn is down >50% compared to this time last year and is the lowest since early 2023. If we were not investing in up / down market / making meaningful R&D bets we could bring that number lower.

*Positives:* **

NRR at 99% for Q3

Serious enterprise opportunities are emerging that could be game changers. We brought on Ann Dale to continue our momentum on the enterprise side of things.

COGs continues to improve with <$.40 per 1K page view cost. For reference, we were at $.61 in Feb and close to double that last year. This is good because our margin will get better as we scale too. IE if we double traffic our server bill will only go up a bit and cost per page view will drop meaningfully.

Patent received on Edge Deliver Engine + AI content optimization system (advised we will receive) + patent filed on EdgeID

20%+ close rate / general funnel metrics look solid besides fully loaded SQL costs

Removing account management saved a lot of $ / is resulting in a lot better NRR

Burn is down >50% compared to our peak and lowest since early 2023. Sept has some 1 times costs that will spike it but generally we are burning less money since before Signal Peak fina

updates.subject: Nostra Q3 Investor Update
summary: Quarterly update

updates.subject: Nostra Investor Update Q2 2025
summary: Investor update from Nostra
updates.rawText:
Hi all,

Q2 was a mixed bag—growth was flat, but we posted our best quarter yet for Net Revenue Retention (NRR), including ~100% NRR in June. That’s a strong signal that our core product is delivering real value and that our move upmarket is beginning to pay off, even in a difficult macro environment. In Q3, we’ll begin rolling out upsells—including RootID, bot protection, server side analytics and LLM optimizations—and I’m bullish we can continue to build on this NRR momentum.

Over the past 12 months, we made a deliberate decision to cut GTM spend from $450K/month to $100K/month. Combined with moving to only annuals, tariffs and consumer headwinds, it’s not surprising we struggled to sign new brands during this period. But this was intentional: rather than acquiring customers who would churn, we focused on building products that deliver real, differentiated value and selling to buyers who could really afford the product. As expected, this led to a high burn without growth.

That strategy is now starting to have a glimmer of positivity. The product is better. The unit economics are stronger. And while burning capital without growth is not good, it was necessary to fix our leaky bucket.

We’re now at a fork in the road:

Stay quiet and wait until Q1 to re-engage the market while continuing to burn $300k/mo, or

Begin acquiring customers again now, ahead of holiday and increase burn for 4 months to $375k/mo.

Given the early signs on NRR and product strength, we’re choosing to re-engage. This means burn will increase in Q3 as we invest in top-of-funnel efforts to validate our improved product suite and accelerate new customer acquisition. We increased our GTM spend from $100k/mo to just shy of $200k/mo as we would rather get feedback that things are working / not working a couple months early rather than wait until mid next year to get a read on the market. This spend will take the form of media spend, conferences and a head of marketing (who we recentl

updates.subject: Tildei Q3 Investor Update
summary: Close enterprise customers, reaching $500K in ARR by EOY
Build pipeline to position us well for Q1 2026
Scale analytics, automation, and onboarding for enterprise readiness
Strengthen infrastructure and observability under Naveen’s leadership
HIRE and build out our team, specifically with GTM
updates.rawText:
Tildei - Q3 2025 Update

Executive Summary

This past quarter was a pivotal one for Tildei — marked by strong
product-market validation, new capital, the addition of a world-class CTO,
and measurable customer impact. We’re executing against a clear mission:

“Tildei is an AI agent platform for brands that transforms scattered brand
knowledge into intelligent, actionable conversations across every customer
touchpoint. Unlike basic chatbots, Tildei agents complete tasks—building
carts, booking appointments, and capturing zero-party data through natural
conversation.”

I encourage you to watch our updated pitch (here
<https://app.fyxer.com/call-recording-snippets/bVEh19l3pC4UYkjyiCBf>),
which reflects how we’re communicating our value to customers today. The
snippet comes from our recent pitch with Ingenuity Brands
<http://www.ingenuitybrands.com/>.

As we enter Q4, I’m excited to share meaningful progress across product,
revenue / customers, and team — and to officially announce our new SAFE
funding, which positions us to accelerate execution and momentum into 2026.

Thank you for your continued support and belief in what we’re building.

Our primary investor ask continues to be help with recruiting — you’ll find
details and linked JDs for open roles below.

Best,

Mark

__________________________________________________________________________

New Funding — $2M Bridge to Accelerate Growth

We raised $2 million in new funding last quarter on a YC SAFE capped at $35
million, led by existing investors Susa Ventures and Ludlow Ventures.

The purpose of this bridge round is to hit the gas on growth — enabling us
to:

   -

   Bring on our new CTO, Naveen Akunuri (more below)
   -

   Expand our engineering team to build faster and strengthen product
   velocity, and
   -

   Invest in go-to-market with our first dedicated enterprise sales team


This capital gives us the operational runway and confidence to execute

updates.rawText:
Dear Investors,

As I mentioned in our H1 update last week, we have been very focused on
pivoting Tildei to be the system of record and intelligence for global
brands. Today we shared the news with the world. You can find my LinkedIn
post here
<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/activity-7354258646966435841-Z7SD?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAAqV7IBpwU5-UQpyPaRcabiFA3gRifUnF0>.
Please share, like and introduce us to brands. We are hitting a nerve and
its time to dominate.

PS, we also went live with our new website <http://tildei.com>, and I wrote
a blog post here.
<https://www.tildei.com/post/a-note-from-our-ceo-building-the-marketing-system-of-intelligence-where-your-brand-knowledge-comes-alive>


Thank you for your support!!

Mark

--

To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an email to investors+unsubscribe@tildei.com.

updates.subject: Tildei H1 2025 Update
summary: Investor update from Tildei H1 2025 Update
updates.rawText:
Tildei H1 2025 UpdateExecutive Summary

As many of you know, our original thesis, which was exclusively focused on
WhatsApp lifecycle marketing, did not gain the traction we anticipated.
Since the start of 2025, we have successfully pivoted to building Tildei as
the marketing AI system of record for global brands. We believe that
mid-market and global brands consistently struggle with ensuring
consistency across their customer communications, experience, and internal
reporting. To address this, Tildei is now an AI-powered marketing platform
that creates intelligent brand agents for commerce and marketing
conversations. We achieve this by building a comprehensive, custom Brand
Knowledge Graph from your product catalog, marketing materials, FAQs, and
brand guidelines. These agents are then deployed across social and digital
channels to engage customers 24/7 in any language, guiding them toward
product recommendations and purchases, essentially acting as your brand's
most knowledgeable sales associate.

We are actively expanding to web chat, SMS, and other channels, already
helping brands like FabFitFun, Mall of America, JomaShop, and Toys"R"Us
turn customer inquiries into revenue-generating conversations. Q2 marked
our first quarter selling the new product, and we are pleased to report
closing $72K in ARR, demonstrating early market validation of our thesis.
Q3 is also off to a strong start with pricing out to Vimeo, American Eagle,
upsells with Cariuma, and Sephora currently in legal. As I type this, we
just closed a Big Snow Ski for $14.4K to power their Meta channels only, I
expect us to cross $100K in ARR by the end of July.

You can watch a brief demo of the new platform here: Tildei Platform Demo
<https://www.loom.com/share/03d83fcdadc145bf92bdee1efc9449e9?sid=ac38874f-b388-4460-98d5-d79655808168>
.

We will be launching a new website in the next week or so and currently
have a teaser <https://www.tildei.com/> live. This new site

Dear PYM Advisors & Investors,

It's a pleasure to share PYM's Q3 update with you all. The quarter reflects
the discipline and focus we’ve been building toward all year. PYM continues
to strengthen fundamentals — growing revenue, cutting burn, and setting the
stage to lead one of the most dynamic opportunities in mental wellness and
longevity: owning the stress category.


**We’re aiming to close the remaining $275K of this round by year-end to
maintain the strong growth momentum across all channels. We welcome support
from our network to complete the raise within this tight timeframe and
appreciate any warm introductions.*


PYM Q3 2025 Investor Update

Snapshot:

   - Gross Revenue: $429K (+.01% QoQ | +48% YoY)
   - Burn: $199K (↓34% QoQ) |↓57% YoY)
   - Fundraise: $1.725M raised of $2.0M | $275K remaining

Challenges:

updates.subject: PYM Q1 2025 Investor & Advisor Update: Continued Growth in a Volatile Market
summary: Although we wanted to prioritize closing our round prior to officially
updates.rawText:
Dear PYM Advisors & Investors,


It's a pleasure to share the PYM quarterly update. We held this update
until we could fully gauge the impact of new global-supply-chain policies
on our operations. Fundraising discussions remain active, but broader
market volatility has stretched some timelines. On the bright side, revenue
keeps rising, our pipeline is robust, and the team has successfully
neutralized most tariff-related cost pressures. Key updates are as follows:


PYM Q1 2025 Quarterly Update

Q1 Highlights

   -

   Gross Revenue: Q1 @ $403,458 (5% YoY growth) | April @ $195,893 (17% YoY
   Growth)
   -

   Retail Velocities: Increased Natural channel velocities by 10% QoQ
   -

   Retail Expansion: Opened up east coast DCs to expand natural channel
   doors by 50+
   -

   Shopify Growth: Increased revenue by 25% YoY
   -

   Amazon Growth: Increased revenue by 50% YoY


Q1 Lowlights

   -

   Tariffs: However, our team proactively planned for potential tariff
   impacts and expect less than a 2% increase in product COGs—allowing us to
   protect margins and stay on track toward profitability in 2025.
   -

   Lost retail doors in retail transition: Based on PYM’s trade spend
   analysis
   <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1t2P_yUfNDrDeVwvHlGP1Cclbhwa6zyGKMNWvyNCldlI/edit?usp=sharing>,
   we transitioned off shelf at Target and The Vitamin Shoppe. We remain
   partners with Target selling online with a recent launch of Magnesium Stick
   Packs on Target.com in Q1.


Q2 Outlook
Plans to scale growth with secured funding.

   -

   Close Fundraising: Raising the final $200k of this round by the end of
   May to support omni channel growth to scale revenue to ~$3M in 2025.
   -

   Revenue: On track to have the highest quarter in the history of PYM with
   stable retail fill rates and scalable D2C channels.
   -

   COGs Reduction: Reduced with updated supply chain on Mood Biotics, Mood
   B Complet

updates.rawText:
Snif Investors, Stakeholders & Friends of the Brand,

We hope you're having a great week! We're excited to share that our new Milk + Cereal collection is now available nationwide in all Ulta stores and online at Ulta.com ( https://www.ulta.com/p/milk-cereal-layering-bundle-pimprod2058508?sku=2655736 )

The collection features our newest milk-inspired EDT called 2% with a fresh dairy accord, lactones, milk carton accord and vanilla, along with three mist pairings: Lucky Streak (m arshmallow ), Crunch Time (m elted butter, cinnamon sugar) and Frooty Call (lemon sugar, strawberry)

The collection is currently featured on a tower at Ulta, giving it major visibility during launch week. This is a big retail milestone for us, and we'd love your support in helping get the word out. Here are a few easy ways to get involved:

* Buy Milk + Cereal in-store at your local Ulta ( https://snif.co/pages/store-locator )

* Shop online at Ulta.com ( https://www.ulta.com/p/milk-cereal-layering-bundle-pimprod2058508?sku=2655736 ) and leave a review

* Like, comment and share our latest Milk + Cereal posts on Instagram ( https://www.instagram.com/p/DW9Z1f2if3s/?hl=en&img_index=1 ) and TikTok ( https://www.tiktok.com/@snif/video/7626829530547653901 )

Thank you for the continued support!

Best,

Will

---

*Will Duda*

Vice President of Finance

snif.co ( http://snif.co )

(612) 462-0872

will@snif.co

updates.subject: [Snif] Investor Update - Series B Closing
summary: Investor update from [Snif]
updates.rawText:
Snif Investors,

We’re thrilled to share that Snif has officially closed our Series B financing round, led by a group of both new and existing world-class CPG investors. This marks an exciting new chapter as we continue to bring Snif’s fun, levity, and refreshingly anti-traditional approach to fragrance into new products, new categories and new channels.

With this new capital, we’re doubling down on our investment in people, marketing, brand awareness and product innovation, while continuing to scale our retail presence and support long-term growth.

Thanks so much for the support, and please be on the lookout for a 2025 YTD performance update in the coming weeks.

Best,
Bryan

Bryan Edwards
Co-Founder, Snif
www.snif.co ( http://www.snif.co )

updates.rawText:
Hi Nik,



Happy New Year!



This past year was truly a record breaking year for us here at Pepper! Hope
2025 was as good to you as it was to us – filled with friends, family, and
lots of recipes inspired by our community.



*Here’s what we're most proud of and a look into what 2026 will bring…*





 [image: 79e71617-bf7d-448f-bf5c-dfafabba5d97]* YoY Metrics:*

   - 26,542 P+ subscribers (+784%)
   - $1.3M P+ ARR (+1,119%)
   - $82.5k Paid Partnership Revenue (+254%)
   - 110.2k MAU (+94%)



  [image: 7fe3c79f-7d47-40ab-93d8-7f43aca627d4]* Highlights:*

   - *Pepper became a profitable business with $1M+ Annual Run Rate (ARR)*
   in subscriptions alone and over 25k current paid subscribers! While our
   active usage naturally grew with enhanced product offerings, our
   subscription skyrocketed after optimizing paywalls and hyper-targeted ads.
   - *Growth Milestones*
      - *ARR grew **+1,119%**YoY* as we reinvested profits back into
      growth. More excitingly, we're finally arriving at our first cohorts'
      renewals, which means compounding revenue in the new year –
rather than the
      current dynamic of free trials being the sole indicator of P+ revenue.
      - *Conversion from Download to Free Trial grew **+102%** YoY *to 13%
      while conversion from trial to paid subscription stayed strong
at 50%. Our
      Conversion to Paid is an incredibly high mark for a consumer social
      business, especially just 1 year into launching a subscription.
      - *Charcuterie packs opened grew **+727%** YoY* to 650,703 as we
      launched new Leaderboard & Challenge features.
      - *Newsletter scaled to 140k+ active subscribers *with a 35% open
      rate. Our newsletter will be a material engine for new subscribers & paid
      partnership opportunities in the new year.
      - *Hired Ellie Harris to lead Social Media & content*. Since joining
      in September, she has created multiple posts with ove

updates.rawText:
Non LP - MAGIC Fund UPDATE (Q1-2026) - LP Version
*MAGIC Fund UPDATE (Q1 - 26)*

Hey Nik,

Q1 reinforced a trend we’ve been watching closely: while capital continues
to flow aggressively into frontier AI, founders building outside that race
are operating in a far more selective environment where differentiation
matters more than ever.

For companies building outside the infrastructure layer, differentiation
increasingly comes from distribution, customer insight, and community. We
explored this idea further in *Curation and Community in an Age of
Convergence* and continue to believe that as technology becomes more
accessible, the companies that win will be the ones people keep coming back
to.


Until next time,
Goke and the MAGIC Team

*A cross-section of MAGIC General Partners*

**** [CONFIDENTIAL] ****


*Our Portfolio Companies are Growing Larger*


Two (4) of our portfolio companies completed major capital events:



   -

   🇨🇴 *Somos Internet
   <https://magic.mxmagnoilia.com/61a79a672eb76825c1090023/l/b0j1KY8K4OeSBIyJx?rn=&re=i02bj5SYpRWZtFWbyFGazB0ap5mI&sc=false>,*
   a Medellín-based internet service provider, raised a $40M Series B to fuel
   its launch into Mexico City — its first international market outside
   Colombia — alongside continued build-out of FiberX, a data-center-inspired
   fiber network.
   -

   🇺🇸 *Pirouette Pharma
   <https://magic.mxmagnoilia.com/61a79a672eb76825c1090023/l/Qv4XdPfnhbduo59ap?rn=&re=i02bj5SYpRWZtFWbyFGazB0ap5mI&sc=false>*
   raised $4.9M on Wefunder. The capital will expand headcount and fund the
   build-out of an AI co-pilot for buyside traders — a new product line for
   the institutional trading platform.
   -

   🇺🇸 *Proof Trading

updates.rawText:
Hi investors!


We’re officially live with MALKA, a brand I’m incredibly proud of.


About a year and a half ago, I converted to Judaism and took on my Hebrew
name, Malka, meaning queen. The name stayed with me because it represents
strength, legacy, responsibility, and quiet power.


It inspired me to design for the modern queen. The woman balancing,
building, carrying, nurturing, and leading. Hands full, head held high.


So I created Malka: elevated phone accessories designed to move with you
through everyday life.


The timing feels especially meaningful as I prepare to enter motherhood in
just a few short months. Malka is for the woman doing a lot, carrying a
lot, and still showing up every day with grace.


See the website here. <http://markofmalka.com> Please send me your address
and desired phone chain for yourself or your significant other.



*Updates*



   1. *Launch snapshot:*
      - We had a popup on Abbott Kinney in Venice, CA for launch with the
      brand 12th tribe. <https://www.instagram.com/shop12thtribe/> (671k
      followers on Instagram)
      - $7K revenue in week one with zero paid marketing
      - 25% of initial inventory sold
      - COGS already covered
   2. *Sales & Marketing *
      - Sales continue to be strong post launch (seeing min of 2 orders a
      day)
      - Influencer seeding launches next week to test organic lift
   3. *Product*
      - First collection launched across 3 colors and 2 sizes (Garden of
      Eden)
      - Early customer feedback is strong on both functionality and
      aesthetic positioning
      - Exploring partnerships with adjacent brands (Brick, Yondr) for
      inventory acquisition and potential collaborations for old LOOKUP
      inventory.
   4. *Team*
      - Tiana Haraguchi driving influencer strategy and network expansion
      - Two interns are supporting daily content and social execution
   5. *Burn *
      - Capital is sufficient to

updates.rawText:
Hi investors!


Happy New Year! It’s been a little while since my last update, and I’d love
to reconnect 1:1—please feel free to call or text me anytime at
*425-283-7320*.


Over the past few months, I’ve taken time to deeply evaluate LookUp’s
positioning, traction, and long-term scalability. That work has led to a
clear and exciting strategic pivot that I’m confident sets us up for a
stronger, more focused path forward.


*Key Learnings*

This year underscored that LookUp is a challenging consumer pitch. The
product requires a high level of education and behavior change around phone
usage, which makes DTC adoption difficult.


That said, the product has continued to *sell out at our #1 retailer in
Venice, CA*, reinforcing that LookUp performs significantly better in *physical
retail environments* than online—validating the product itself, if not the
original go-to-market strategy.



*Updates*

   1. *Product*
      - I’ve begun conversations with adjacent brands (e.g. *Brick, Yondr*)
      to explore:
         - Purchasing remaining LookUp Tin inventory
         - Potential acquisitions or collaborations
      - Many of these companies are tech-forward but lack strong branding,
      partnerships, and lifestyle positioning—an area where we’ve consistently
      excelled.
   2. *Strategic Pivot*
   We are transitioning into a new, focused brand called *Malka*, centered
   exclusively on premium phone chains.
      - *Launch:* January
      - *Instagram:* https://www.instagram.com/markofmalka
      - *Brand positioning:*
         - *Malka* means *“queen”* in Hebrew
         - Designed for high-performing women—founders, mothers, creatives—who
         are constantly on the move
         - The product is intentionally dual-purpose: functional utility +
         personal style
      - Apple’s recent launch of phone chains further validates demand and
      category momentum.
      - Unlike LookUp’s broad SKU set, Malk

updates.subject: Hetal Monthly Update - Apr 26
summary: * To our supporters, investors, and friends,
Store Coverage (Unique Stores)
100 customers. Yup. LFG! Fun fact: first customer was a Kardashian (Lemme) and the 100th was too (Update).
Great sales month/quarter. Mentioned above >$400k FFARR and ~$1.35M for Q1, which exceeded our stretch goal by ~$100k.

80 activated stores per week. I noted in the last update, but we’re pushing on coverage now. We’re building strong learnings for acquisition and are confident there is still a ton of low hanging fruit to pick off from a product / marketplace dynamic side.
New CVAI onto production. We’ve hit a few more hiccups deploying the newest models onto production. We’re now better leveraging our DevOps contractor to help here. Should be on in April.
Grader speed. New CVAI will help speed up the graders further, so our grading has less lag + is even more cost efficient.

** To our supporters, investors, and friends,
------------------------------------------------------------

March was another super strong month. >$400k in FFARR (contracted revenue). Brought Q1 to ~$1.35M FFARR, which is ~$100k above our $1.26M stretch goal. Fwiw, we have higher plans in Q2, but I am interested to see if there’s any seasonality in the business (lots of sets go live late Q1 / early Q2), so Q1 might just be particularly strong for us.

We are working hard on all fronts: Sales, Product, Hetalite Coverage, Acct Mgmt, etc. I alluded to it in last month’s update, but Expo West was amazing. So many customers would just stop us to say thank you. It really was so powerful/meaningful.

Let’s dive in, but always have to start with a song (https://open.spotify.com/track/02Jfe2Wl9MGcKPVfFZyj4K?si=c0b272ce4da445fd) .

Key Metrics
* Sales / GTM
+ ARR (Proj) - $1,580,440 (LM: $1,235,588)
+ Contracted ARR - $2,660926 (LM: $2,208,638)
+ Revenue = active + pre-booked - churned
+ ARR = Contracted ARR * Actual/Max Ratio
* Store Coverage (Unique Stores)
+ Hetal Stores Audited - 43,034 (LM: 33,429)
+ Total Max Stores - 72,455 (LM: 59,755)
+ Coverage Rate - 59.4% (LM: 55.9%) (Q1 Goal = 72%)
* Spend Efficiency
+ Spend = $6,856 (LM: $6,391)
* Notes
+ ARR (Proj) = Contracted ARR * Hetalite Coverage. This ends up being lower because we don’t always cover every aisle for every brand. Example: brand has 1 or 2 aisles in a store. We audit a store w/only 1 aisle. The price per store is different.

Highlights
* 100 customers. Yup. LFG! Fun fact: first customer was a Kardashian (Lemme) and the 100th was too (Update).
* Great sales month/quarter. Mentioned above >$400k FFARR and ~$1.35M for Q1, which exceeded our stretch goal by ~$100k.
* 80 activated stores per week. I noted in the last update, but we’re pushing on

updates.subject: Hetal Monthly Update - Aug 25
summary: * To our supporters, investors, and friends,
Store Coverage (Unique Stores)
Session Activity (L4W, Total Instances)

Strong hiring. With our hiring of Hunter (https://www.linkedin.com/in/h-park/) , we’ve completed this phase of hiring. We’re now in a strong spot to support Hetalite activity, grow sales, and develop our CVAI/AI capabilities faster & better.

Dispatch reliability. We set an initial goal of merchandising 66% of the requested stores. Though it just launched, we want to hold ourself to a high standard. Two weeks in and our coverage is ~55%. Still work to do, but we’re learning and have confidence we can surpass our goal.
Walmart launch. We are preparing accordingly on all three key areas that this touches: tech, operations, and sales. Our goal is to launch in Q3.

Dispatch improvements. We’re learning a ton here, both in terms of what customers want from our technical product and how we can improve reliability (i.e. # of merchandised stores desired vs visited). Lots of work to do.

** To our supporters, investors, and friends,
------------------------------------------------------------

Slow for no one! We’re executing at a high level across the board. Our two new hires are hitting the ground running and we hired an amazing candidate to fill our CVAI need. We finally launched Dispatch and are starting to learn from our customers. We’re building out the pipeline and closed some of our biggest deals to date (launching Aug & Sep). We’re rethinking the Hetalite system so that we can improve reliability and thereby grow low-hanging revenue. We’ll expand to Walmart this year, are prepping for that operationally, and signing existing and new customers pre-launch. Lots and lots happening.

Let’s dive in, but first a song (https://open.spotify.com/track/0mAfnHKGAYxQ71qK4rCfqn?si=ef65bb8cf04e419b) (got famous for their cover of Linger, which is unreal).

Key Metrics
* Important Note: We will update to unique stores starting next month. You will notice Session Activity appears to be down, but that’s only because we released a new Frequency product aimed at improving our data margin. Since we only charge for unique store audited per month, we essentially give free audits for any store that’s already been audited that month. Frequency lowers audits per store from 2x per month to 1.5x per month. So while the overall activity is less, the unique store activity is still in line.

updates.subject: Hetal Monthly Update - Jun 25
summary: * To our supporters, investors, and friends,
Store Coverage (Unique Stores)
Session Activity (L4W, Total Instances)

New website. We launched the new website (http://hetalretail.com) at the EOM. Just another step in preparing for launch. We will build out the Use Cases section with actual full-fledged Case Studies in the coming 1-2 months.
Fundraise. Need to close! Hopefully we’re on a last turn and then first close on Fri Jun 13. As I mentioned, a bit tight but still the target date.

Hiring. (1) Hetalite Marketing, (2) CVAI + General AI, and (3) Sales. For sales this includes an AE to do BDR + CSM and potentially a sales coach. We already brought on a contractor for RevOps as we’ve switched fully onto Hubspot (previously we were using a spreadsheet). More in Your Help.

Hetalite coverage. Already discussed in Obstacles, but this is a major focus for us in Q3. We’re leaving money on the table by not covering as many stores. We’re also losing out on data margin now that a strong set of stores have enough customers to be sustainable.

** To our supporters, investors, and friends,
------------------------------------------------------------

We signed a term sheet from NVP shortly after last month’s update. Ludlow will be coming in as well. The two will contribute almost 90% of the entire round ($3.5M/$4M). We’re now working through a few turns before deal docs are finalized. Our target close is this Friday, June 13. It’s a bit tight but we’re still aiming for it. The entire round is committed with finalized amounts (thank you everyone who was willing to take less so we could keep on building an amazing cap table!).

With the new funds, we’re already starting to build out the infrastructure (tools and bandwidth) needed to scale further. This includes a variety of business units like sales, Hetalite marketing, VR operations, and product (CVAI, AI, dashboard, and app).  More in Your Help for ways to assist.

updates.rawText:
Hey all,

*If you're getting this email, you're part of
a short list of friends & colleagues that I respect tremendously. As
a solo founder of a 3-person 7-person
company, I'm going to start sending monthly updates to you all to hold
me accountable and
to learn to ask for help when I need it. The e-commerce community has been
nothing but kind to me, and I appreciate you all being a part of my journey
growing The Starters.*

*And I get it—inboxes are way too cluttered these days. If you ever want to
be taken off this list, just let me know—no hard feelings at all.*

*****

You may have noticed I stopped sending these monthly updates about four
months ago.

The reason is simple: when things are going well, these are easy to write.
When things get hard, they're not.

In December, I made a big bet. One that will either unlock what The
Starters can truly become, or at minimum give me the clarity I need to know
what this business is and isn't.

But before I get into what we're building, I want to share some personal
context.

My entire life, I tied money to success. And for a long time, that equation
seemed to hold. Every promotion felt like a life upgrade. When I landed my
first six-figure job, I leased my first Porsche. When I was promoted to CMO
and took Winc public, I bought a house. Both felt like wins. Both made me
happy.

When I left all of that to build The Starters, with a fat car and mortgage
payments to still make each month, it was hard.

Then over the last 2 years, for the first time, I got a real taste of the
kind of financial success I'd always chased. The Starters was (is) thriving
and very profitable. My consulting work was (is) strong with minimal time
commitment. I got married. By every measure, things were great.

And yet, I felt a void. Something was missing.

I spent a lot of time trying to understand what. Sleepless nights. Zoning
out in the middle of conversations. Constantly wondering to myself what
a

*****

*November Overview*

November was a tough month and the core business regressed 2% MoM.
Acquisition completely stopped over the last ~5 days of the month as brands
hunkered down for BFCM. That, coupled with worse than normal churn, led to
our first down month in a very long time. That being said, I'm keeping my
head down on continuing to improve the product offering + our marketing
efforts and feel good heading into 2026.

In November, I was focused on:

   - *AI Implementation: *We're working hard on implementing
   customer-facing AI tooling at The Starters to help brands find talent
   faster. We're testing these tools internally and hope to launch something
   on the platform by the end of Q1.
   - *Video Content:* If you've seen my LinkedIn posts lately, you'll
   notice a lot more video content. I'm testing this out as a strategy
   to bring our message to life in a more fun way + establish trust with our
   prospects. While videos haven't performed well from an impressions
   standpoint, I do think they'll be an important bottom of funnel conversion
   lever on LinkedIn and could help spread top-of-funnel as I activate
   video-first platforms like Reels, Shorts, and TikTok.

   flow, improved quiz funnel results, and improvements to our boutique agency

*And I get it— inboxes are way too cluttered these days. If you ever want
to be taken off this list, just let me know— no hard feelings at all.*

*****

*October Overview*

October was an up-and-down month. It started soft, but rebounded in the
last week and we managed to eke out 3% MoM growth on the core business. It
was also the first month of having our Boutique Agency Program live
(piloting 20 boutique agencies to be listed alongside our freelancers).
It's shown strong traction for about ~70% of participants.

In October, I was focused on:

   - *Content:* I've engaged 2 freelancers (yes, from The Starters) to help
   support our organic content strategy across SEO, organic social, blogs, and
   case studies. In October, we nailed an SEO strategy, I started
   incorporating some ghostwriting on my organic social, and posted a few
   short-form style videos.
   - *Events: *As part of my "word of mouth" enablement growth strategy, I
   hosted 2 small community events in October— one in LA and another in
   Chicago. Both were great and I'll continue to host small events from time
   to time.
   - *Digital Product: *We launched dynamic ranking of freelancers on The
   Starters. This incentivizes talent to keep things on platform + respond to
   brands on time in exchange for a higher ranking on the platform.
   - *New Roles: *We're now accepting talent across Finance, Opartions,
   Retail, and Product verticals, which marks our expansion outside of core
   e-comm enablement functions for the first time.

Honestly, things are going really smoothly. The Starters is not a
"rocketship" SaaS, and it never has been, but we continue to grow slowly
month over month and that compounds over time. We've also been able to get
much, much better at digital product and roll out new features quickly +
with less bugs. For those of you who have been following these updates
closely, you know that's been a massive issue in the past.

In September, I was focused on:

   - *Boutique Agency Program:* In last month's update, I shared that we
   were piloting a program for boutique agencies to list themselves on The
   Starters. We had an overwhelming response, with 50+ agencies asking to be a
   part of it and only 20 spots for the pilot. I spent September vetting +
   onboarding these agencies, along with pushing the digital product
   initiatives to support it. We officially launched yesterday, and I'm
   excited to see how it goes. This program won't end up being a
*massive *chunk
   of our revenue, but it could grow to about ~20% of the business and support
   our brand members even better... a win-win.

   for us so far... enabling word-of-mouth and referrals organically. For me,
   that means spreading

recap. Over the last 2 months, ARR grew >10% and we are trending around 60%

marketing. I'm focused on smart investments we can make to grow faster.

      quality are both at all time highs.

      our Quiz Results page. You can check it out by going through our lead
      funnel quiz here <http://hirethestarters.com/quiz> (you'll be asked
      to create an account; use a different email if you already have one)
      - We're also working on "Smart Ranking" - aka ranking Starters in
      search results based on their performance (responsiveness, reviews, $
      billed, etc). This should help surface the best Starters first, and

updates.rawText:
Hey all,

*If you're getting this email, you're part of
a short list of friends & colleagues in the space that I respect tremendously.
As a solo founder of
a 3-person company, I'm going to start sending monthly updates to you
all to hold me accountable and
to learn to ask for help when I need it. The e-commerce community has been
nothing but kind to me, and I appreciate you all being a part of my journey
growing The Starters.*

*And I get it— inboxes are way too cluttered these days. If you ever want
to be taken off this list, just let me know— no hard feelings at all.*

*May Overview*
Last month, cash receipts grew by a staggering 22.8%. While ARR just grew
~5%, we were able to grow cash receipts significantly thanks to 2 new
initiatives: 1) Converting frequent users into our annual plan and 2)
offering 1:1 profile consulting sessions to our Starters. These initiatives
are allowing us to significantly increase revenue and profits without
needing to increase prices for our core membership.

Last month, I was focused on:

   - Piloting our 1:1 profile consulting offer– this took up the bulk of my
   time as I wanted to ensure the Starters that paid for this service got
   excellent results. So far, so good.
   - Digital Product: I wish I had a better update to share here, but we
   encountered several new (but minor) bugs on the platform due to trying to
   do too much at once. We're slowing down our pace of new initiative launch
   to ensure the platform performs as expected across the board and the
   features that do release are bug free.
   - Exploring an offshore talent beta. Shoutout to Sam @ Black Wolf Nation
   for the push here; we're piloting an offshore talent solution in
   conjunction with Tidal; more to come here soon
   - Establishing a process to convert frequent users to our annual plan.

updates.rawText:
Hey all,

*If you're getting this email, you're part of a short list of friends &
colleagues in the space that I respect tremendously. As a solo founder of a
3-person company, I'm going to start sending monthly updates to you all to
hold me accountable and to learn to ask for help when I need
it. The e-commerce community has been nothing but kind to me, and I
appreciate you all being a part of my journey growing The Starters.*

*And I get it— inboxes are way too cluttered these days. If you ever want
to be taken off this list, just let me know— no hard feelings at all.*

*April Overview*
Last month, core business ARR grew 3.3% MoM. While this was short of
expectations, we had a huge wave of involuntary churn brought on by 4x
higher than usual credit card declines in March. Customer acquisition
rebounded and was very strong in April, allowing us to continue to grow
despite the wave of involuntary churn.

Last month, I was focused on:

   - Digital Product
      - We have completed development on a new feature that allows Starters
      to initiate projects on-platform, which should reduce
disintermediation and
      improve overall retention
      - We are just about complete with our job posting + application
      revamp. This has been a long project with several delays, but the finish
      line is in sight.
   - Improving our tech partner program
      - Some of the changes we made to our tech partner program actually
      turned out to work against the effectiveness of the program; I spent time
      in March talking to our partners and implementing updates to our
processes
      to improve ROI
   - Re-doing customer onboarding
      - I've previously tried to automate or hand-off the first 30 day
      brand onboarding work, but this has backfired each time. In April, I took
      this back on and we've been seeing a huge uptick in hire success
rate as a
      result.
   - Launching a referral program
      - I shared

updates.rawText:
To our esteemed investors,

Thanks for everyone's patience with this — please find our *2025 FY
Recap* report
below.

*Overview*

In 2025, we grew the Kolkata Chai brand while making significant
improvements to our business fundamentals. We dramatically improved our
margin profile, moved towards profitability, and leveraged digital content
to grow category awareness.

We improved EBITDA by 63% and Gross Profit by 7.7% YoY, establishing a
scalable foundation. This was enabled by cutting operational expenses,
moving to a 3PL and achieving lower COGs on key raw materials.

Total revenue fell below expectations due to a decline in DTC, where we
pulled back marketing spend by 32% YoY due to cash constraints. Given the
difficult fundraising environment, we chose to conserve cash rather than
chase low-quality revenue. However, we doubled Foodservice/wholesale
revenue, which we believe has significant upside in 2026.

We are incredibly optimistic about 2026 due to our new Chai Concentrate
product, foodservice/wholesale and retail expansion, DTC growth,  brand
collaborations and celebrity-backed activations.

To get back to growth, we need additional capital and are seeking
introductions to friends and family, family offices or CPG-focused
investment funds actively deploying capital.

*Click here** for the full update. As always, please keep this information
confidential*: *2025 Q4 + FY recap
<https://kolkatachaico.notion.site/2025-Q4-FY-recap-321bdd2d60dc8085bb8dedd7cd1e9955>*

*2025 Revenue*

*2025 Highlights: *

   - Blended Gross Margin (all channels): 57%, 7.5% improvement YoY *(COGS,
   cost of shipping, freight, delivery and processing fees are included in GM)*
   - eCom contribution margin (after advertising): Generated $502,397,
   which was a 50% improvement YoY
   - EBITDA trendline: Compared to losses of $1,149,065 in 2024, we
   improved EBITDA by 63%, giving us a line of sight to profitability on our
   core channels

To our esteemed investors,

Hope this finds everyone well. We’re sharing our progress so far this
quarter and including an additional ask about our current fundraise below.


*Mid-Q3 updates:*

   -

   *eCommerce momentum:* Since May, our eCom channel is +24% YoY (including
   +22% on DTC) reversing the softness from Q1. We believe this performance
   will continue due to new product launches, healthy repeat purchase
   behavior and customer acquisution improvements
   -

   *Tea Bag Launch:* We introduced our 3-SKU chai tea bag line, to remove
   the friction of making our chai and increasing the occasions that people
   can interact with our products. It’s quickly become one of our top selling
   items in the last 4 weeks of soft launch. Try it for yourself here:
   https://kolkatachai.co/products/masala-chai-tea-bag
   -

   *Profitability progress:* We improved our Net Operating Income
   performance in June by 45% YoY, continuing the trend from months
prior and approaching
   breakeven. June was our best NOI performance in the past 18 months,
   driven by opex savings from our cuts in Q1, improved advertising efficiency
   and tight cost management
   -

   *Cafe revamp strategy:* We’re rolling out a limited-time, collaborative
   menu with Hasan Minhaj in September as part of our café revamp (expanded
   food menu, more seating, refreshed experience and service) to restore
   topline revenue numbers
   -

   *Major partnership alert:* We’re debuting our partnership with Amazon
   next month at their Accelerate conference in Seattle, with multiple
   activations, a national press release and broad content push

Feel free to share the fundraising blurb below with people in your circle
to help accelerate our path to closing the $750k we're looking for. We are
20% committed thus far and gaining momentum every week.
—

*Kolkata Chai Co brings healthy, authentic chai to the masses through our
cafe, eCommerce and foodse

updates.rawText:
To our esteemed investors,

Please find our 2025 Q1 Recap report within. We had a challenging quarter
due to political and economic uncertainty, shaky consumer sentiment and
general growing pains. Given this environment, we made the difficult
decision to sacrifice revenue growth in order to rightsize the business,
cut costs and focus on product innovation to drive the next phase of
growth. These decisions have had an immediate effect on the P&L and we
remain confident in our approach for the rest of 2025.

Click the link below for the full update. As always, please keep this
information confidential.

The full report available *here
<https://kolkatachaico.notion.site/2025-Q1-Investor-Recap-1debdd2d60dc80948d90d278301bb848?pvs=4>*
.

*2025 Q1 Overview: *

   - Total revenue decreased by 20% YoY, due a prioritization of improving
   profitability and cash flow over revenue growth
   - Maintained profitability across our brick and mortar locations despite
   a challenging operating environment
   - Finalized R&D, formulation and launch for 3 new "retail ready" product
   formats — tea bags, liquid concentrate and an instant powder
   - Reduced operating expenses by ~40% by cutting headcount, agencies,
   software, warehouse lease and more
   - Improved net income by 50% from January to March as a result of opex
   cuts

*Revenue & Traction*

*Retail Readiness *

We're developing new product formats with the goal of launching in
retail/grocery by late 2025-early 2026. We have a meeting with Whole Foods
tomorrow at 11am and will keep everyone posted. 🤞

*Bridge financing*

We are raising a bridge round of $500k that will be used for purchasing
inventory for our new product formats, increasing runway and preparing for
a regional retail launch. We're looking for $25-$100k checks from existing
investors — please contact Ani for details if interested.

*Cap Table Migration Complete *

updates.rawText:
Hi Esspo investors, Happy 2026. Wishing you all a caffeinated and
possibility-filled year ahead.

It's go time. We're rolling out *nationally at Whole Foods in April* and
launching *digitally next month*.

In Q4, we hit a snag: two SKUs were foaming at room temperature. We turned
it into an opportunity—ran giveaways, let people in on the problem, built
community around solving it. We fixed the problem, and the delay actually
strengthened our launch lineup and brand voice.

Below are headlines from the final stretch + specific ways you can help us
launch strong.

   -

   February digital launch: Pushed launch by a month because of carbonation
   issue—solved by switching co-mans and batching orders.
   -

   Doubling down on afternoons: By early afternoon, consumers hit a wall:
   They don’t want more of the same coffee, they don’t want a jittery energy
   drink, they’re not ready for alcohol. Afternoons run on Esspo.
   -

   New lead SKU: Classic Vanilla replacing Dirty Chai for broader appeal.
   Updated all formulations based on feedback about sweetness and coffee
   intensity.
   -

   WFM activation locked in: Presenting marketing plan to Whole Foods this
   month. Three-pillar strategy (retail, digital, brand) focused on top ~150
   high-velocity stores in SoPac and Northeast.
   -

   Office drop-ins: Organized over a dozen drop ins in Q4 including Reddit,
   Raine, Hearst, Bustle.
   -

   Building community on social: Experimented with messaging to understand
   what’s resonating on social (example
   <https://www.instagram.com/p/DRP39iGAR8m/?igsh=MWZmYTVmcWdrcjRnZg==>,
   example <https://www.instagram.com/p/DRfATCxgMJ_/?igsh=eDN3dHkwbWxnNGc=>).
   Lots of traction with early giveaways leveraging foaming cans—hundreds of
   people reaching out (see below) / early press proactively seeking us out.

------------------------------

HOW YOU CAN HELP

We have a 90-day Whole Foods exclusivity window before we ca

updates.rawText:
Hi Esspo Investors,

Sharing our first company update - a lot has happened and things are moving
fast so we wanted to loop you in on where we stand and where we could use
your help.

The Headlines:

   -

   Rebrand complete & production ready — first production run is in August
   with a ready-to-ship timeline in October
   -

   Wholesale momentum — we’re in active planning with Whole Foods for a
   rollout in 2026. We’ve also partnered with Critical Mass Group, the most
   influential and selective broker in the space
   -

   Our Ask — We are targeting to close another $500k by October and would
   like intros to smart investors like yourselves who back breakout consumer
   brands.  Please make introductions - Thank you!

------------------------------

We completed our *brand overhaul *finalizing the transition from Mad
Duchess to Esspo:

   -

   Name: Esspo. Simple logic - Espresso + pop. We're creating a new
   category, so we need a name that does some education work for us. Like how
   Poppi signals prebiotic soda. Esspo signals both coffee, carbonation, and
   refreshment.
   -

   Positioning: Espresso soda. Two words that tell you what it is - drinks
   like a soda, caffeinates like espresso. Plus, this is how coffee shops are
   already describing it organically, which makes adoption easier.
   -

   Design: Goal was education + shelf standout. The can design shows the
   soda-espresso fusion and captures our brand energy with an elevated logo in
   power red. We tested two directions with 200 consumers - 90% picked this
   one, so we feel good about the choice.

[image: Screenshot 2025-07-23 at 1.12.03 PM.png]

(I've attached our full brand book - if you're interested, it'll give you a
good sense for how we're planning to bring Esspo to life in the coming
months.)

------------------------------

Production: Our first production run is this August with a ready-to-ship
timeline of October.

   -

Hey Nik!


Sharing the updated investor information for the next iteration of Mad
Duchess. I know you had to jump through a few hurdles with this process -
thanks for bearing with us and staying on board as an investor.


We have you marked down for an investment of *$8,143* - the amount returned
from Mad Duchess to your Angellist account; if for any reason you want to
invest more we’re here for it. We’re aiming to close by *next Wednesday,
May 28*.


This next chapter is a smarter, stronger step forward. Over the past year,
we’ve immersed ourselves in the CPG space—speaking with dozens of experts
across wholesale, digital, operations, and product. Every conversation has
helped us get clearer and more confident in where we’re headed. But above
all, what’s made us most excited is how we convinced even skeptics the
minute they tasted the product.

updates.rawText:
Hello Friends of Hiyo,

The year got off to a pretty unforgettable start on my end... I had the
privilege of marrying the love of my life on January 31st! In between
wedding planning and celebrations, the Hiyo team has been hard at work
kicking off what is shaping up to be a transformational year for the brand.

Here are a few highlights from the first two months of the year:

   - *A Dry January Takeover 🚀: *Dry January was not quiet for Hiyo. It
   was a cultural takeover. Between 40K field marketing samples, a Dry Jan
   party beneath our first LA billboard, 2.45M+ PR impressions, and 18.7M OOH
   impressions, the brand showed up everywhere people were thinking about
   drinking differently. We capped it off with a huge milestone: our national
   launch into Costco.
   - *Costco Coast to Coast 🛒:* Speaking of Costco, January marked our
   national rollout across more than 400 warehouses in all regions. To think
   we started with just 20 warehouses in Denver and San Diego last July makes
   this moment even more surreal. The rapid expansion is a testament to strong
   performance and the incredible customer feedback we received once Costco
   members discovered Hiyo. Huge shoutout to our Ops and Sales teams for
   producing, coordinating, and shipping almost 120K Sunset Party Packs, our
   exclusive 18ct Costco variety pack, in record time. It's safe to say the
   club channel is just getting started for us.
   - *Leveling Up Online 💻:* Behind the scenes, we rolled out two big
   upgrades to our digital experience: a brand new B2B ordering portal and
   enhanced rewards and bundles program on our website. These tools make it
   easier for our partners to order Hiyo directly while giving our community
   more ways to stock up on their favorites and cash in points for exclusive
   merch, helping build deeper brand equity. We will be monitoring AOV over
   the next few months to see if these initiatives create the expected uplift.
   -

updates.rawText:
Hello Friends of Hiyo,

2025 was one of those years that changed everything. From new flavors and
new shelves to unforgettable moments on festival grounds and stages across
the country, we felt the full weight of what we’re building together.
Instead of highlighting just what occurred in December, I wanted to switch
things up and tell you about the milestones that we're most proud of from
2025.

But before I dive into that list, I want to start off by detailing a HUGE
announcement and *our ask of you and your community*...

*We’re thrilled to share that our 18-ct Sunset Party Pack has officially
landed in Costco nationwide!*

This is a huge milestone for Hiyo and a testament to the growing demand for
the category that Hiyo is leading. Ahead of our broader announcement on
January 7th, we’re kicking off a friends and family push to help drive
early momentum. If you or someone you love shops at Costco, keep an eye out
for the Sunset Party Pack and help us spread the word. This is a huge
opportunity for Hiyo, so any support you or your friends and family can
offer is greatly appreciated! Here is a link to our store locator to find
the closest Costco to you: https://drinkhiyo.com/pages/store-locator

Now onto some milestones for the memory books:

   - *Two New Floaty Flavors *🍊: We grew our fruity, booze free lineup
   with two new favorites this year: Passion Fruit Tangerine in March and
   Pineapple Coconut in July. Both quickly became fan favorites and solidified
   that the Float Fam loves a tropical twist.
   - *Shelves on Shelves on Shelves * 🛒: 2025 was a *breakout *year for
   retail. Hiyo landed nationwide at major chains like Target and Whole Foods,
   with our social tonics now stocked in 6,000+ stores across the country.
   Next year we're eying 10K+!
      - [image: image.png]

Hello Friends of Hiyo,

September was all about scaling, activating, and setting the stage for a
big back half of the year. From breaking records in wholesale to kicking
off our first big commercial campaign and expanding retail wins, here’s
what we’ve been up to.

Whether you're an investor, vendor partner, Hiyo community member, or Hiyo
superfan, you're receiving this "Friends of Hiyo" email because at some
point, you asked to be updated on all the happenings in Hiyo-land. We hope
these emails will be informative, entertaining, and bite-sized. We're happy
to share with you, but please keep these emails *confidential*.

Let's start the highlight reel:

   - *Live Nation Takes Center Stage 🎶: *In September, we activated 40
   Live Nation venues in major cities nationwide after closing our exclusive
   partnership deal in August. This is a transformative win for Hiyo, giving
   us unparalleled access to millions of culturally engaged consumers at the
   very moment they’re seeking elevated, non-alc options. This partnership
   embeds Hiyo directly into the live music and nightlife ecosystem, cementing
   our position as the go-to functional alcohol alternative for social
   occasions and where the best memories are made. It not only accelerates
   trial and awareness, but also reinforces our leadership narrative with
   retailers and distributors by demonstrating cultural relevance and consumer
   demand. Most importantly, it establishes a scalable flywheel that
   competitors cannot replicate, strengthening both brand equity and category
   leadership.
   - *Happy In Your Own – First Look 🎥:* We just wrapped production on our
   very first big-budget commercial campaign celebrating what it means
to be *Happy

   October, but as a Friend of Hiyo, you’re getting the first look. Click in,

updates.subject: Postscript Advisor Update - March ‘26
summary: Investor update from Postscript Advisor
updates.rawText:
PS Advisors,

Hope all is well! 2026 is off to a bang and we’re due for an update here.
First, a few highlights from ‘25:

Business Results - We ended 2025 101% to our revenue goal and 115% to our
new business bookings goal, on much tighter cost controls than originally
modeled as we entered the year. We’re continuing to grow nicely while
getting radically more efficient. Postscript is on a 9 figure run rate and
message volume 65% grew year over year. We will be profitable in 2026 👏

Obviously it’s not all rosy–much of the industry had a tough year in ‘25,
and we felt that too. But I’m proud of the team for rallying through those
headwinds and delivering solid results.

Amazing brands moved to Postscript in 2025! To highlight a few: Mejuri,
Aviator Nation, Boll & Branch, Raycon, Instant Hydration, Little Words
Project, Diff Eyewear, Kitsch, OUAI, Birddogs, and many more. They came
because they can maximize the channel with Postscript in ways they can’t
with other providers–via list growth, revenue per message w/ AI, cost
savings, and the best strategy+service motion in the industry. We’re so
glad to be partnering with them all.

Innovation Continues - We continue to lead the category on the two vectors
that matter most in SMS performance: opt-in infrastructure and agentic
messaging.

   -

   We remain out in front on Onsite Opt-in (OOI). It drives meaningful list
   growth without the compromises to conversion (keeping the users on site) or
   attribution (starting a new browsing session) that two-touch and two-tap
   do. We also continued to build the underlying infrastructure, doing our
   first license deal to Alia and launching OOI-Engage which overcomes any
   rumored (but honestly, yet to materialize) impact from iOS26 and the
   unknown sender folder.
   -

   Our product with the most market pull right now is Shopper. Shopper is
   our agentic system powering 1:1 sales conversations while staying on brand.
   This is

updates.subject: Final Update on Very Good Light & good light cosmetics
summary: Investor update from Final Update on Very Good Light & good l
updates.rawText:
Dear Investors,
I'm writing to share a final update regarding Very Good Light and good light
cosmetics.Over the past several months, we pursued a potential sale of the
company in earnest. We engaged with multiple interested parties, shared detailed
financials, and went through an extensive diligence process.
As recently as last week, we were still working toward a possible transaction.
Unfortunately, despite genuine interest and significant effort on all sides, we
were unable to reach final terms.
While this isn't the outcome we hoped for, I want to express how proud I am of
what we built together. Your belief in this brand allowed us to create something
truly meaningful.
Because of your partnership:
We grew the business meaningfully over the past few years, expanding into major
retail and strengthening our presence in the market. We operated with financial
discipline in a challenging environment, reaching EBITDA-positive performance
this year (for the first time in company history). We built a community-driven
brand centered on inclusivity, sustainability, and care for our customers.
Building this company has been one of the honors of my lifetime. I am deeply
grateful for the trust you placed in me and for the support you showed our team
and our mission.
In consultation with our advisors, we will now proceed with an orderly sunset of
the business. Our priority is to manage this transition responsibly, including
selling through remaining inventory and completing the formal dissolution
process in accordance with our obligations.
This will be my final formal update. I've also written a personal essay on this
journey and the community we built, which will be published in ALLURE  tomorrow.
Thank you again for believing in good light and for standing with us along the
way. I will always be grateful for your partnership.
With so much gratitude,--
David Yifounder/ceogood light  & Very Good Lightauthor of PRETTY BOYSCEW's
Hottest Indie Brand 20

It’s been a while. I’m writing from a Dunkin’ Donuts in Seoul, where Michi and I

you’ve all been taking care of yourselves.

Summit with Meta, we launched our latest innovation: Strawberry Moon Grinding
Cleansing Balm, which is having a moment both on social media, but in the news
and on shelves at ULTA!​​

PRESS COVERAGE HEREE! NewsEBONY​ALLURE​
We were also the main subject in a story forBusiness of Beauty. Read ithere.
To be transparent, this year has been a lot for everyone, and the last few weeks
have been some of the hardest of my career.
I’ve been deep in the trenches trying to keep this brand going and find a real
path forward. If you’ve walked into certain ULTA doors on a weekend, you may
have seen me there, selling products myself and talking to customers. Beauty
Beyond the Binary still matters to me as much as the day I wrote it, but we’re a
team of three, and the challenges we’ve faced recently have been enormous.
Here’s what happened:1. Our warehouse transition was bumpy. Shipping new
products was slow.
This led to incorrect shipments, delays, stock outs, and a ship rate at ULTA

The silicone tip swells shut, making the product unusable. ULTA has said the

flow to absorb. We are urgently pushing our manufacturers here in Seoul for
accountability.

Dearest Investors,
Hope you're all doing well and taking care of yourselves. It’s been a whirlwind
year, and I’ve been head's down trying to make this company a success. These
days you can even find me working retail part-time on weekends, as I'm selling
good light and meeting customers at a few ULTA doors! It's that determination
and passion that I feel I still have for this company – and I want to do
everything I can to build a lasting brand.
I’m proud of how far we’ve come, even through the turbulence of
tariffs, political turmoil and backlash against DEI, and the economy. One thing
that hasn’t changed? My commitment to our mission of Beauty Beyond the Binary—a
mission that’s more important now than ever.  
Let’s be real—this hasn’t been the easiest time to champion inclusivity. With
DEI under fire from political and cultural forces, it’s been daunting. But I’ve
never done this work because it was trendy. I’ve done it because it’s right. And
I’ll continue standing up for the people who need us most—because our community
deserves it. Thank you for believing in me and backing this vision.
As we moved into 2025, I’m proud to say we didn’t just survive—we evolved. We’ve
found ourselves at the intersection of three powerful movements: clean beauty,
the K-beauty wave, and the growing demand for inclusive products. Through
strategic decisions and relentless hustle, we've set good light up for what I
believe will be our strongest year yet.
PRESS:Read more here fromBeauty Independent if you haven’t already done so.
Harper's Bazaar named our toner "Best Milky Toner"  2025.Catch me in Vogue

marketing yet is the top brand in their category, the only wellness shot
selected by Starbu

updates.rawText:
*End-of-Year Update*

Hi all —

Hope you’re well. 2025 was a *transformational year* for Disco. We
fundamentally reshaped the business — moving from a Shopify-centric supply
model to a much broader distribution footprint across enterprise retailers,
consumer apps, and B2B2C SaaS platforms.

At the start of the year, *Shopify represented ~99% of our traffic*. Today,
it represents *~30%*, with overall distribution growing *more than 5×* into
new environments.

We landed major enterprise retailers including *Knitwell Group* and *Duluth
Trading Company*, expanded into consumer applications like *GoPuff* and
*Mindbody*, and began rolling out a new solution — *OfferOS* — for B2B2C
SaaS platforms (e.g., Narvar, Seel, CommentSold, Mindbody). These partners
enable Disco to monetize critical post-purchase and post-transaction
moments by making our platform *composable and API-led*.

As a result, Disco now operates across the post-purchase journey for brands
like *JCPenney, QVC, DSW*, and many others.

*What didn’t go well*

Early in the year, we made a major structural change: *we transitioned from
not paying publishers to paying them*. This created two clear sides of the
ecosystem — publishers and advertisers — and required us to move all supply
into formal contracts.

This shift was strategically correct but *poorly timed operationally*.

We migrated existing supply to paid contracts in Q1, which immediately
compressed net revenue, while the new value proposition (paid supply) took
longer than expected to unlock new enterprise and consumer-app
partnerships. Most large enterprise deals didn’t sign or go live until
September/October, creating a prolonged timing mismatch between increased
payouts and new supply coming online.

As a result, growth for much of the year was subpar relative to the scale
of the transition.

*2025 results:*


   -

   *$11.0M gross revenue* (+41% YoY)
   -

   *$6.6M net revenue* (-11% YoY)

This t

updates.subject: Covey Business Update & Next Steps
summary: Investor update from Covey Business Update & Next Steps
updates.rawText:
Dear Covey Investors,

We hope you are all doing well. We wanted to share an update on the
business, our recent efforts to find a strategic partner / buyer, and
potential paths forward from here.

Over the past several months, we have run a comprehensive process to
identify an investor or acquirer for Covey. As part of this effort, we
engaged Merge, a consumer M&A advisory firm, to run a structured outreach
campaign on our behalf. While we continue to believe in the quality of our
products and the loyalty of our long-tenured customers, the broader market
environment has posed real challenges, and we want to be transparent about
where things stand.
Market Dynamics & Brand Positioning:

As many of you know, Covey was first conceived in mid-2018. We
intentionally set a high bar for product quality and underwent multiple
rounds of development to ensure we were bringing products to market that
were meaningfully differentiated. The iteration process, across each of our
SKU’s, meant our formulation period took 4 years, leading to a slower cycle
to launch—ultimately in the middle of the Covid pandemic— coinciding with
an explosion of new skincare brands entering the market.

Unfortunately, many of these newer players came to market with
significantly larger marketing budgets, allowing them to build rapid brand
awareness despite offering far inferior products. The DTC skincare
landscape quickly became extremely competitive. In hindsight, we were not
capitalized to a level that would have allowed us to run an aggressive
marketing strategy at the pace needed to fully capitalize on our early
momentum in influencer-driven, clean skincare.

Even so, the business has generated positive cash flow over the prior 18
months and we have seen excellent retention and stickiness among
long-tenured subscribers—validation that the product quality remains a real
strength of the brand.
Process to Find a Buyer or Growth Investor:

To explore alternatives to

updates.rawText:
Dear Covey Investors,

I'm writing to update on our exploration of strategic options for Covey,
either via investment or acquisition. We have been working with Merge, a
firm specializing in capital raising and M&A for companies of our size.
We've had several conversations with different investors and acquirers, but
have not found the right partner yet.

We are currently evaluating next steps and are committed to finding the
best path forward. The company is generating modest cash but still in
decline year over year as we have kept marketing spend to a minimum. If you
know of any individuals or companies that would be a good fit to invest or
partner, please let us know.

Thank you for your continued support,

Emily, Kyle & Christina


Christina Garcia︱Co-founder

updates.subject: Brightland Pre-Holiday Update!
summary: BRIGHTLAND <http://www.brightland.co/> *| @wearebrightland
updates.rawText:
Hello!

As we head into the holiday season, Brightland <https://brightland.co/>is
approaching the end of the year with great momentum — from our Everyday
Line launch at Whole Foods to exciting collaborations and continued retail
wins. We’re feeling energized, grateful, and focused on sustaining this
pace as we grow our presence across new channels and audiences. Thank you,
as always, for being part of this journey and cheering us on through every
milestone.

*Highlights:*

🌿 *Everyday Line Launch:* Brightland launched our Everyday Salad Oil and
Everyday Cooking Oil nationally at Whole Foods in September!

✨ *Whole Foods Trend Report:* Brightland was featured in Whole Foods’
Annual 2026 Trend Report <https://www.instagram.com/p/DP7Hw6AkotM/?hl=en>as
a leader in Kitchen Couture, spotlighting brands bringing both style and
substance to the category.

🍽️ *Co-Hosted Dinner with Whole Foods:* We partnered with Whole Foods to
host an influencer and press dinner in Austin, featuring an olive oil
tasting led by the WFM merchant. This was a first-ever co-hosted event
between Whole Foods and a supplier brand — a huge milestone! The event was
a smashing success.

🛒 *Retail Momentum:* We’re investing in merchandising and demos, and have
seen strong early results — 20–30 units sold per demo in many cases, with
incredible off-shelf displays in sidecaps and produce. We also ran our
first promo, which delivered a notable lift in velocity.

👩‍💼 *Team Growth:* We welcomed our new SVP of Marketing, Stephanie
Chevalie <https://www.linkedin.com/in/stephaniechevalier/>r (previously
leading consumer marketing at MasterClass and with a strong background in
beauty).

📱 *Content Expansion:* We brought on a dedicated TikTok creator to
accelerate growth on that channel.

🐻 *Brand Love:* We introduced our new mascot, the Brightland Bear,
<https://www.instagram.com/p/DPO46RPEeuc/>which has already sparked
excitement and engagement across our community.

*GMA Deals & Steals last week: ( https://www.instagram.com/p/DMeCgcBxsUK/?hl=en )* Our first National TV Coverage on Good Morning America ‘Deals & Steals’. Bringing in $200K Total Sales for our ~48 hour Flash Sale, plus 4,500+ new customers right before our anniversary sale this fall

* 

*Times Square on Mother's Day: ( https://www.instagram.com/p/DIuhVE2s-0o/?hl=en )* Because sometimes you need to remind the world (and yourself) how far you've come

* 

*Nemah x Shopify Soho Pop Up ( https://www.instagram.com/p/DJVd_zUMhu8/?hl=en ) -* In Partnership with Shopify, we hosted over 1000 guests in May for our ‘ Nemah - Momentum Forward’ event in Shopify’s Soho Store. A big IRL moment for the brand

* 

*ROAS up / Marketing Cost Down:* We parted ways with our Agency for Email, SMS, Paid Search, & Paid Social & brought in-house with killer freelancers. Saving ~$2K/month while improving ROAS & Content quality

* 

*Biggest Month Ever (x2)* : While June & July are traditionally some of the worst months in E-Commerce, June was our biggest month ever - $180K Total Sales between Shopify & Amazon, and July just passed $200K!

*The Tough Stuff:*

* 

*We got sued.* ADA Compliance aka Ambulance Chasers for E-Comm came after us. Luckily - we made the move from Accesibe to Patrol (unluckily, it was 3mo too late) to bulletproof our Accessibility comp
