updates.subject: Black Crow AI Update
summary: Push vs Pull (Instagram/Sora vs Google/ChatGPT)*
OpenAI + Shopify: Why It’s Exciting for Us*
AI Storefronts: Proof on the Board*
updates.rawText:
Hi, Dear Investors,

It’s been a  full few months at Black Crow, and I want to share both the
big picture of what we’re building, and the progress we’re seeing with the
AI Storefronts platform I described last time.  (**I also have an ask for
you at the bottom of the note re intros to some specific large ecommerce
brands!)


*The Big Picture*
Over the last decade+, social platforms have industrialized the art of
attention generation: what to show this user, at this moment, to maximize
X.  That could be attention, content creation or, most importantly, ad
consumption and engagement. But the attention industry stops at the click;
once ad engagement happens, clickers are sent to (drumroll)… webpage that
hasn’t changed much since 2005.. The “destination layer” — the surfaces
after the click — has seen almost no innovation, despite being the most
commercially consequential space in the digital world.  Not good.

That’s why we build AI Storefronts. Black Crow is industrializing the use
of attention. We take every ad creative and audience, analyze the intent,
and generate storefronts that match — personalized, predictive, branded.
The goal: turn attention into transactions, and do it at AI scale. We
believe the "destination layer" is the last under-managed surface of the
internet, and fixing it is a very big prize.


*Push vs Pull (Instagram/Sora vs Google/ChatGPT)*
There are really two forms of customer engagement at the top of commercial
funnels–internally we call them “Push” and “Pull”.  Black Crow focuses
mostly on Push today.  It's the very top of the funnel, where intent is
manufactured via commercial messages that are pushed in-feed during passive
content consumption.  We think people will continue to watch funny videos
(whether made by humans or machines) and Push discovery will endure.  The
role of AI Storefronts is clear here.

But there’s also pull commerce: when intent already exists and the shopper
asks the internet for som

updates.subject: Investor update - Black Crow AI
summary: "We’ve now built storefronts for every major product category — bracelets,
updates.rawText:
Dear Investors,

Hope your summer is going well, and you get at least a few days away from
email … but if you’re checking in from the beach or the back seat of an
Uber to JFK, here’s what’s been happening at Black Crow!

For those of you I’ve spoken to recently, you know we’ve been very focused
on building our “AI Storefronts” products.  Even in the last few weeks, the
market and customer validation points have begun to really stack up, and we
believe we’ve hit a juncture when our foundational infra work is paying
off: we think we have the right product, at the right time, for the right
market.  It’s very, very exciting. And now we're just starting to put some
numbers on the board.

For sure it hasn’t been all smooth sailing though.  As you may know, much
of e-commerce has been buffeted by tariff gyrations and uncertainty around
international supply chains.  That’s created headwinds for many commerce
brands, which has translated into more churn than we’d like in our legacy
(ie non-storefronts) base–particularly among smaller, highly
margin-sensitive merchants.  Churn hurts, but has also been a forcing
function for our product and investment focus — and we’ve zeroed in on AI
Storefronts as our development priority and the way we go to market; it
leverages everything else we’ve built to date.

So, what are AI Storefronts?

AI Storefronts are individualized, AI-generated shopping experiences for
each shopper, channel, and moment. They are not landing pages or
templates.  They are fully shoppable micro-environments — personalized,
predictive, and performant — built on our stack of machine-learned
predictions, real-time identity, and commerce-native orchestration.

Here’s what that means in practice:

   -

   When a shopper clicks through from, say, a Meta ad, or a Klaviyo email,
   or an Attentive SMS, our system identifies the context and — if possible —
   the individual.
   -

   Our AI generates and delivers a bespoke Storef

updates.rawText:
*STAND+ 2025 Q3 Recap*
STAND+ just hit our *highest sales month in company history*, while
completely selling out core SKUs, making profitability moves with tighter
operational efficiency, and furthering our wedge as the category leader in
Energy-Recovery Footwear™.

*Performance Highlights*
*Year-To-Date **(Jan–Aug 2025 vs. Jan–Aug 2024)*

   - *106% Increase* in Top-Line Revenue
   - *121% increase* in D2C Sales
   - *215% Increase* in B2B Sales
   - *49% Increase* in Site Conversion Rate

*Month-Over-Month (August 2025 vs July 2025)*

   - *New Monthly Sales Record *-* $437k** (ahead of projections!)*
   - *14% Increase* in Gross Revenue
   - *21% Increase* in D2C Revenue
   - *38% Improvement* in Gross Margin

*Operational Optimizations*

   - *Reduced Discounting* - Due to full priced inventory of AntiGrav1 and
   AntiGrav2 *selling faster than we can stock*, we've made the decision to
   move away from routine discounting and remain full price during most major
   discounting seasons with the exception of BFCM or clearing old styles.
   - *Eliminating Free Shipping* - We rolled back free shipping and somehow
   saw an increase in conversion rate, finding us additional margin on every
   pair sold.
   - *Updated Returns Policy* - Now in line with industry standards, we're
   no longer accepting worn and used shoes, while still offering free returns
   and exchanges within 30 days. This change significantly improves the
   percentage of returns in re-sellable condition.

*Channel Highlights*

   - STAND+ has been added to *Starbucks* and *Home Depot* employee perk
   programs off demand from individual locations for corporate to adopt.
   Conversion rate of employees who've viewed the perk is an astounding 15%!
   - *First Repeat Purchase from NFL Team* with new teams coming on board
   and heavy interest around the upcoming launch of our AG3 slides!
   - Inbound interest from the *US Army*! Clearing National Defense

updates.rawText:
Hellooooooo investors and friends.


Does anyone miss hearing from me weekly??? No? Okay, anyway, I’m here to
report on how Stay wrapped up Q2. Let's start with how YOU can roll up your
sleeves and be a part of this team.

How you can help:

   -

   We’re constantly looking for great brands doing $2-50M in subscription
   revenue and I know you know some of them.
   -

   If you have connections to any of these brands, we’d love an
   introduction or good word: Dirty Labs, Create Wellness, Cymbiotika, Dieux,
   Ultima Replenisher, Canyon Coffee, Wild Earth, Ghost Lifestyle, Every
   Man Jack, Spindrift, Heights, Qui, InnoSupps, Open Farm, Orgain, Native,
   Kleeq

Highlights:

   -

   Live ARR is sitting around $12M
   -

   Cash burn is tracking significantly better than Plan and we expect to
   exit this year close to breakeven with over $3M in the bank.
   -

   Onboarded over 160 merchants so far in 2025 totaling $1.6M of ARR

Lowlights:

   -

   Generating outbound pipe has been a challenge for our AEs and BDRs over
   the past few quarters. The good news: we've brought in strong new
   leadership and fresh talent, and early signs point to real momentum
   building in our outbound motion. We’re excited to see that translate into
   stronger pipeline in the second half of the year.

Team Updates

   -

updates.rawText:
Hi All,

I wanted to take a moment to introduce myself as Stay's new VP of Finance.
I look forward to providing you with regular quarterly updates on both our
financial performance and business developments. Below, you'll find our
latest insights for Q1.

*Highlights*:

   - Live ARR is currently sitting close to $11M
   - Cash burn is tracking slightly better than Plan and we expect to exit
   this year close to breakeven with $2M+ left in the bank.
   - Onboarded over 100 merchants so far in 2025 totaling over $1M of ARR
   (last year, we did not hit this milestone until the end of July!)
   - Kicked off a data warehouse project with Hop Labs to enhance our
   analytics and custom reporting capabilities and set us up for rapid AI
   innovation.

*Lowlights:*

   - While Marketing and Partnerships have been gaining momentum, our AEs
   and BDRs have struggled to generate pipeline. If you partner with
   any companies that have successfully optimized their outbound motion, we’d
   greatly appreciate introductions or insights into what’s worked well for
   them.

*Team Updates*

   - Arash (Director of Sales) exited Stay in mid-May and we’re actively
   searching for a Head of Sales. If you know anyone who might be a good fit,
   please let us know.


*GTM (Marketing, Sales, Partnerships)*

   - 50% win rate on both Yotpo introductions and partner sourced pipeline
   in Q1
   - Exceeded sales quota for signed ARR (147% attainment)
   - Launched our new website which led to a 237% increase in the Book a
   Demo form conversion rate and a 49% drop in rage clicks.

updates.subject: Hundred Days New Offering: Volume Edit Page Campaigns
summary: $2,500 campaigns for $1.00 CPM or less (2.5M+ views)
$5,000 campaigns for $0.75 CPM or less (6.67M+ views)
$10,000 campaigns for $0.50 CPM or less (20M+ views)
*Laufey - "Silver Lining"
*Jonah Kagen - "God Needs The Devil"
*Doechii - "Anxiety"
*Young Thug - "Money On Money"*
updates.rawText:
Hi Friends,

Hundred Days is excited to introduce a new offering - *The Volume Model* -
a high-volume edit page strategy that will quickly deliver dozens to
hundreds of posts in targeted categories at an improved CPM.

To accomplish this, we've onboarded nearly a thousand edit pages to a new
platform where we pay our creators based on the performance of their posts,
rather than a fixed*, *predetermined rate, as with our *Traditional Model*.

Under the Volume Model*,* our campaign CPMs have been exceptional. We've
delivered:

   - $2,500 campaigns for $1.00 CPM or less (2.5M+ views)


   - $5,000 campaigns for $0.75 CPM or less (6.67M+ views)


   - $10,000 campaigns for $0.50 CPM or less (20M+ views)

Unlike micro-creator UGC campaigns that typically feel like spam, this is a
targeted offering within impactful edit page categories such as Film/TV
Pages, Lyric/Sped Up Accounts, Quote/Aesthetic Pages, and Sports Edits. The
Volume Model offers the same level of quality that you've come to expect
from a Hundred Days campaign, but now at scale.

Here are some example campaigns that we've run with the Volume Model:

   - *Laufey - "Silver Lining"
   <https://dashboard.hundreddays.co/client/32-28>* (Quote Campaign - 20.4M
   views across 85 posts)


   - *Jonah Kagen - "God Needs The Devil"
   <https://dashboard.hundreddays.co/client/jonah-kagen-god-needs-the-devil-discord32>
*(TV
   & Film Campaign - 8.3M views across 36 posts)


   - *Doechii - "Anxiety"
<https://dashboard.hundreddays.co/client/32-38> *(Lyric
   Campaign - 4.3M views across 58 posts)

   - *Young Thug - "Money On Money"*
   <https://dashboard.hundreddays.co/client/32-69> (Sports Campaign - 2.5M
   views across 99 posts)

We're extremely excited about this offering and are here to answer any
questions you may have. Feel free to reach out if you have any campaigns
that you're interested in running through our new model.

-- 
Best,

Ben Klein

Preside

updates.subject: Brunch — A little update
summary: Here’s some highlights from the last 6 months:*
On the Growth side:*
Looking ahead we plan to:*
Refresh our website and dial in our eComm strategy, including investing deeper into paid
Refine our marketing & merchandising mix to be inline with our plan of providing elevated, comfort footwear
Open our first NYC Flagship and deepen our presence in the city we love
We’re looking for help with:*

updates.rawText:
Hey Brunch Fam!

If you’re getting this, you’ve played a part in helping me and my brother Daniel on our journey building Brunch. We’re so grateful for the support, and we wanted to loop you in on the updates.

This is the first of occasional updates where we’ll share what we’ve been up to, spark ideas and ask for guidance. Any and all feedback is always welcome and appreciated.

*Here’s some highlights from the last 6 months:*

We improved our merchandising significantly by extending our core Essential into new colors, materials and kids. We also dropped Amour ( https://brunch.us/products/amour-suede-shearling?variant=44409673220324 ) , a romantic take on our best-selling Essential slipper. Also — here’s ( https://www.instagram.com/p/C6NHUDOp0d_/?img_index=1 ) Jalen Brunson wearing them pre–Game 3 of the playoffs. Let’s go Knicks.

We introduced some killer collabs with Eggo Waffles, The White Lotus, Beverly Hills Hotel – All of which have been a success and going forward we’re going to double down on some of the elements that make them special – Nostalgia, Hospitality, Luxury. Here’s Patrick Schwarzenegger in our robe for Vanity Fair. Yes, clothed. ( https://www.instagram.com/p/DGyUPZ-p4sg/ )

We saw some cool influencers showing love— Emilie Kiser, Olivia Jade, Jennifer Fisher ( https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/folders/1hAYgHCv8YaLGM4o1k1_jL9nyKTLtbvsM ) — and we’ve been using a platform called Shopmy to track their affiliate sales, which has been awesome!

*On the Growth side:*

Our sales grew 80% from 2023 to 2024. Our YTD sales compared with 2024 are up 180%. And our April YoY sales were up 300%. We’re proud of the growth so far.

After testing a wide range of channels, we now have a clear view of what drives growth. We're entering a new phase, focusing our energy on a few key initiatives to scale intentionally and sustainably.

*Looking ahead we plan to:*

1. Refresh our website and dial in our eComm strategy, including investing deepe

updates.subject: Q1 2025 Nunona Investor Update: Scaling Up, Staying Lean, and Looking Ahead
summary: Krista Maas de Villiers*
updates.rawText:
Hi everyone,

We’re off to a strong start this year at Nunona. As we move through Q2, I
wanted to share a few key updates from Q1, it’s been a quarter of real
progress, strong signals, and laying the groundwork for the next phase of
growth.

Performance Highlights

   -

   We’re on track to hit profitability in June and reach our $1M run rate
   by July.

   -

   Revenue is accelerating: April grew 43% over March, and May is pacing
   75% higher than April.

   -

   Our updated pre+postnatal formula, officially launched in Q1, overcame
   early algae and greens flavour challenges and is resonating. Retention
   cohorts are already performing 10–20% stronger than our original product.

   -

   Conversion rates are also rising, supported by updated messaging, better
   targeting, and design tweaks across the site and marketing funnels.


Operational Advancements

   -

   We are in the process of transitioning to a new co-manufacturer, cutting
   production costs by ~50% and securing retail-ready credentials.

   -

   With this will come a move to a new 3PL partner, which will drive down
   fulfilment costs and improve operational efficiency.

   -

   These shifts, along with a new growth partner and retention expert
   onboard, create the margin and operational structure we need to scale
   Nunona into a household name.


Market Challenges & Pricing Strategy

We’re closely monitoring inflation and tariffs. Thankfully, most of our
ingredients and packaging are domestically sourced, and where they aren’t,
we have U.S. options to switch to if needed.

At the same time, we’re exploring how to bring our price point under
$100/month to expand access while preserving margins. We’re currently:

   -

   Running consumer research on a smaller serving size that maintains full
   micronutrients but with fewer calories

   -

   Testing a pilot formulation to ensure the flavour holds up

   -

   Modelin

updates.rawText:
*With a never ending effort to stay in front of each other…*

* I w anted to share a couple things we’ve been working on.

* Equally curious to hear what’s new on your end.

* Goal being to spark introductions and/or collaborations.

*About a year and a half ago, we brought on Alison Servi as our third partner.*

* She was previously leading global insights and strategy at a 1,200-person agency, working across brands like LEGO, Nike, TOMS and Dollar Shave Club.

* The first thing she said when she got here was that we had to bring Jane Och with her, who now leads our growth team.

*Together they’ve built our Consumer Insights practice at Herman-Scheer* that pairs deep strategic thinking with the best research technology.

The focus is to identify where and how brands can actually grow.

*A couple examples:*

*Best Buy*

Best Buy came to us with a bigger question than performance. How do they stay relevant as the way people shop for technology fundamentally changes?

We designed and fielded a large-scale consumer research program spanning thousands of respondents across the US, combining qualitative shopalongs, in-depth interviews, and quantitative survey work to map the moments that matter most in people's lives with technology. The output is a strategic framework that helps their teams identify where Best Buy has real relevance, where the opportunity to earn it still exists, and how activate against both going forward.

*SusieCakes*

SusieCakes came to us with two questions: why are some markets performing and others not, and where should they expand next?

To answer that, we fielded on-the-ground consumer research across four key markets, Austin, San Antonio, Nashville, and Oklahoma City, conducting shopalongs, non-buyer interviews, and 30+ consumer intercepts per market.

The ouput is a market-by-market growth playbook that gives their leadership team a clear picture of what drives performance, and a framework for evaluating and pr

summary:
To our shareholders,

As we distribute K-1s for 2025, I wanted to share a brief update on the
business and where we’re heading.

When I stepped into the CEO role in January 2025, I had no idea ho
updates.rawText:
To our shareholders,

As we distribute K-1s for 2025, I wanted to share a brief update on the
business and where we’re heading.

When I stepped into the CEO role in January 2025, I had no idea how big,
fast-moving, and foundational the transition would become. What began as a
leadership change quickly evolved into a full-scale rebuild of how OSMO
operates, sells, and scales.

In 2025, we made a critical shift from operating through a distributor to
becoming a direct vendor with Walmart. At the same time, we expanded beyond
finishing salts into a broader salt and seasoning portfolio, launching five
SKUs nationwide.

This decision proved to be the right one. Units per store per week nearly
doubled year over year, while we reduced retail pricing from $8.97 to $6.97
and improved margin structure at retail. We became more competitive, more
scalable, and better aligned with how modern consumers shop.

In 2025, we developed Magic Sauce, Nick’s unique take on hot sauce, and
sold the vision directly to Walmart in Bentonville. That moment set the
stage for a 2026 national rollout, with Magic Sauce hitting more than 3,500
stores in the coming weeks.

Alongside this, we reintroduced our finishing salts as the Chef Collection,
featuring Nick’s signature and premium design with glass packaging. This
allows us to maintain a high-end DTC experience while clearly segmenting
our retail and direct channels.

We also successfully launched on Amazon, where our seasoning bundles and
Chef Collection have quickly become a meaningful and growing revenue stream.

Looking ahead to 2026, we are building on this foundation with significant
expansion. We secured a nationwide rollout with Kroger, the largest grocery
operator in the U.S., and Walmart expanded our footprint following the
success of 2025. We will also introduce our Roasted Garlic SKU into both
Walmart and Kroger in the coming weeks.

At the top of the funnel, Nick continues to be an extraordina

updates.rawText:
Hi everyone,

We closed Q1 with our strongest performance to date and a clear line of
sight to the next stage of growth. We are raising a $1mn seed round to fuel
this.

Here is a look at where we are, where we are headed, and how you can help
us get there.

Performance Highlights

   -

   Q1 revenue grew 250% over the same period last year.
   -

   We are at a $1M+ run rate and continuing to accelerate.
   -

   Retention remains strong. Our subscription base is healthy, and we are
   heading into Q2 with further platform improvements designed to deepen
   loyalty ahead of our new product launches.

Operational Advancements

   -

   Manufacturing remains our most important near-term unlock. We have been
   in a holding pattern at our current facility and have had two manufacturing
   trials since our Q3 update that we’ve had to walk away from. We are still
   making the bites by hand, which impacts both our margin and our capacity,
   but we are close to a solution.
   -

   This month, we are trialling with a new co-manufacturing partner who has
   the automation capabilities we need to scale without compromising the
   product experience.
   -

   We are transitioning to a new subscription platform in April. This will
   bring more advanced tools for loyalty, rewards, and retention optimisation,
   and positions us well ahead of our 2026 product launches.

Acquisition and Market Insights

   -

   CAC remains healthy, with strong payback and a high-value customer
   experience continuing to drive organic lift and word of mouth.
   -

   Our real food, non-nauseating format continues to be our most powerful
   differentiator. It solves a pain point that synthetic prenatals cannot.

Product Pipeline

Our 2026 roadmap includes up to four new SKUs completing our maternal
nutrition platform across pregnancy and postpartum. Three functional food
stacks aligned to core needs, and one format innovation for our r

Hi everyone,

Nunona continues to gain momentum. Q3 marked another breakout quarter, both
in topline growth and foundational scale. We're expanding our product suite
and locking in the infrastructure to scale.

Here’s a look at where we are, where we’re headed - and how you can help us
get there.
*Performance Highlights*

   -

   *Q3 revenue grew 50% over Q2*, continuing the compounding acceleration
   we shared in our last update.
   -

   Our updated retention offer and onboarding journey are delivering higher
   AOV at acquisition and stronger LTV downstream. We've been optimising
   post-purchase touchpoints and have significantly improved subscriber
   retention. (Happy to share numbers in follow-up.)

*Operational Advancements*

   -

   We’ve officially hit maximum capacity at our current facility, and we’re
   thrilled to share that we’ve secured the right co-manufacturing partner to
   scale with.
   -

   Production with our new partner begins in November. They're equipped to
   match our artisanal format today and support future automation, preserving
   quality and unlocking future margin gains, while setting us up
   for meaningful efficiencies into 2026.
   -

   We've just launched a new website, and our new subscription platform
   launches in Q4, bringing advanced tools for loyalty, rewards, and retention
   optimisation.

*Acquisition & Market Insights*

   -

   CAC remains healthy, with strong payback, and a high-value customer
   experience driving organic lift and word of mouth.
   -

   Our real food, non-nauseating format continues to be a top
   differentiator, solving a pain point that synthetic prenatals can't (and
   often cause).

*Business Development & Brand Growth*

   Our influencer and affiliate channel is growing, and the clinical

   dietitians and doulas.

   We’re formalising our Medical

updates.subject: Checking in from Blume
summary:
Hi Nik,

It has been a minute! Hope you are well! We just signed with our lead
investor for our Series A and we are looking for some strategic angels and
family offices to round out the round. As
updates.rawText:
Hi Nik,

It has been a minute! Hope you are well! We just signed with our lead
investor for our Series A and we are looking for some strategic angels and
family offices to round out the round. As a reminder:

Blume is on a mission to build a kinder wellness world. Over the past three
years, we’ve grown revenue by 450% profitably. Last year we reached $32M
CAD in sales and are on track for another 50% growth year driven by Costco.
We are extremely capital efficient with only $2.5M to date raised in 2022.

Blume operates as a truly omnichannel brand, available in 6,000+ doors
across Canada and the U.S., including Whole Foods, Sprouts, Costco,
Walmart, Save-On-Foods, and Indigo and with over 24,000 subscribers across
Amazon and DTC. This fundraise will fuel our U.S. expansion and enable
continued innovation in products that help women fill their cup with
rituals that lead with taste.

Would love to connect with you to walk you through our story a bit more or
see if there is a fit or anyone in your network who may be interested in
participating.

Best,
Karen

-- 


KAREN | (She/Her)
Co-Founder, CEO
itsblume.com

In the spirit of truth, healing and reconciliation, we acknowledge that The
City of Vancouver is situated on the unceded traditional territories of the
xʷməθkʷəy̓əm (Musqueam), Sḵwx̱wú7mesh (Squamish), and səlilwətaɬ
(Tsleil-Waututh) Nations.

updates.rawText:
Dear GrapevineAI Shareholders,

In addition to Caroline's note below, we are excited to share an important update.

In Q4’25, we signed a term sheet for the sale of GrapevineAI to a PE-backed digital marketing company. After running a sale process in 2025, we believe this transaction is the right path forward. The deal gives GV access to complimentary capabilities ,  improves GV’s sales footprint, and delivers a strong outcome for all stakeholders.

*Key terms of the transaction include:*

* 

*$25M total transaction value* (10x our 2025 plan, though it landed at 8x+ due to business performance)

* 

$21M of upfront consideration

* 

All cash transaction (there is $2M of rollover, but only required for me/caroline)

* 

From this upfront cash amount, ~$2M will be carved out for management transaction bonuses, in addition to customary banker and legal fees

* 

A small portion (~5%) will be held back for standard working capital and indemnification escrows

* 

$4M future management earnout

* 

Reserved for Caroline and other key managers

* 

Will be based on 2026 business performance

* 

Shareholders will not participate

* 

The earnout structure and management bonuses were a key part of both securing this outcome and ensuring a successful transition to new ownership. For clarity and transparency, I am not participating in any transaction bonus or earnout (no conflicts)

For investors, this transaction—inclusive of distributions—represents more than a *2.5x return in just over three years, equating to a 30%+ IRR.*

None of this would have been possible without Caroline’s entrepreneurial leadership, David’s robust operational systems, and the dedication of the entire GV team. I’m incredibly grateful for your faith, trust and support over the last 3 years. While the Ampush exit was formative, building Grapevine has helped me fully step into my own entrepreneurial capabilities. This transaction will also serve as a

updates.rawText:
*This is an update for our investors & advisors only - please do not
forward*
Dear Partners,

Happy New Year! I am excited to share a full 2025 recap with you.

Thank you for your continued support throughout the year. 2025 was our most
ambitious yet, and while tariffs threw us some major curveballs, we came
out of the year a stronger business than we entered. Not only was 2025 a
historic year for Caraway, but my wife and I welcomed our second child,
Nora, to the world in October!

While the numbers speak for themselves, I am most proud of all the amazing
foundation building initiatives we were able to accomplish to put us one
step closer to achieving our mission to create the first generation of
non-toxic homes. All credit goes to our world class team.

Given the length of this update, I am attaching this comprehensive review
of the year as a PDF to this email. This includes key highlights across
business performance, marketing, product, operations, channel expansion and
team.

As always, feel free to reach out to me directly with any questions or
comments and I look forward to updating you again after Q1.

Best,

Jordan
--

jordan nathan / founder & ceo

t: 201.618.8143 <201.618.8143%22%20%5Ct%20%22_blank>

e: jordan@carawayhome.com

updates.subject: Kevin Lee - 2025 Friend Update
summary: Friendships / Community*
updates.rawText:
Hi Friend,

Happy New Year! I’m late getting this update out because life is very different now with 2 kids and lots of extended family to celebrate with during the holidays.

If you’re getting this friend update, you’re someone in my life who I want to stay friends with for a very long time.

I don’t get to see all of you that often anymore and my hope with this friend update is to keep you up to date with what’s going on in my life until I see you next again.

There is no tl;dr and this could take a long time to read. This is what I would share with you if we were catching up over a long coffee. So brace yourself.

–

Most of you know me as the forever optimist. If something “bad” ever happens, I usually look at the person closest to me and say with a huge grin, “Nice! Using up my bad luck for the year!”

At one of Josie’s close friends' wedding this year, we closed out the night saying goodbye to the groom. In his drunken state, he grabbed me by the shoulders and slurred, “Kevin, I feel like you’re one of those guys who I could just slap across the face and you’d still be smiling.”

It would’ve been a nice comment, except I’ve probably exchanged fewer than 50 words with him over the past 4 years so apparently that’s my entire vibe.

Sidenote: Our couple’s therapist says more good things tend to happen to positive people so take that you toxic positivity haters.

But even with my optimism, 2025 really wore me down.

My annual reflection template asks, “What word best summarizes this past year?” Usually I pause, reminisce on the highlights and happily type something like grateful or soulful.

This year, I heard Leo’s reverberating shrieks upstairs and typed CHALLENGING in all caps before moving on.

*Family*

In preparation for our growing family, we made the full circle move from Los Angeles back to Hayes Valley in San Francisco, where we’ll likely stay put for a long time.

7 months into a family of four, we’re now starting to unders

Happy Friday! Wanted to give you a quick rundown of everything that’s
happened in the last month!

   1. We’ve had our highest grossing revenue in Q4 this year, with 202% YoY
   growth
   2. November revenue outperformed our previous all-time high in October
   by +44%

   store experience is thriving
   4. Current December revenue is already +40% higher than our all-time
   high revenue is November with a +78% increase in online conversion rate
   5. We reached 60,000+ Instagram followers

   “Alfré’s Whiskey Christmas” in collaboration with Hillrock Distillery.

   <https://lealfre.com/collections/ties> selling through 65% of the entire
   collection in less than a month
   8. Surpassed 600 customer reviews with an overall rating of 4.97 / 5.0

Also kinda cool… I was nominated as Forbes 30 Under 30
<https://www.forbes.com/profile/brandon-snower/>!
[image: Screenshot 2025-12-19 at 8.59.52 AM.png]

*Stuff we’re working on…*

   - just wait until you see what we have planned.

*Asks*

   - Hiring a book-keeper
   - Hiring a Penthouse Concierge
   <https://www.linkedin.com/hiring/jobs/4342811214/detail/> to take over
   reservations and penthouse shopping experience during the day shift

*Gratitude*

   - Thank you to everyone who supported for a great year. There were so
   many great moments, learnings, failures, wins that have happened. Doing
   all of this with a few part-time employees isn't easy but we've
   accomplished so much and look forward to building out a team slowly and
   strategically to enhance our efforts! All anyone can do is keep going
   and continue pushing the limit of creativity, culture and experience. Great
   things take time. Can’t wait for what’s in store for next year. Have a
   great holiday

updates.rawText:
Hi investors!


Happy New Year! It’s been a little while since my last update, and I’d love
to reconnect 1:1—please feel free to call or text me anytime at
*425-283-7320*.


Over the past few months, I’ve taken time to deeply evaluate LookUp’s
positioning, traction, and long-term scalability. That work has led to a
clear and exciting strategic pivot that I’m confident sets us up for a
stronger, more focused path forward.


*Key Learnings*

This year underscored that LookUp is a challenging consumer pitch. The
product requires a high level of education and behavior change around phone
usage, which makes DTC adoption difficult.


That said, the product has continued to *sell out at our #1 retailer in
Venice, CA*, reinforcing that LookUp performs significantly better in *physical
retail environments* than online—validating the product itself, if not the
original go-to-market strategy.



*Updates*

   1. *Product*
      - I’ve begun conversations with adjacent brands (e.g. *Brick, Yondr*)
      to explore:
         - Purchasing remaining LookUp Tin inventory
         - Potential acquisitions or collaborations
      - Many of these companies are tech-forward but lack strong branding,
      partnerships, and lifestyle positioning—an area where we’ve consistently
      excelled.
   2. *Strategic Pivot*
   We are transitioning into a new, focused brand called *Malka*, centered
   exclusively on premium phone chains.
      - *Launch:* January
      - *Instagram:* https://www.instagram.com/markofmalka
      - *Brand positioning:*
         - *Malka* means *“queen”* in Hebrew
         - Designed for high-performing women—founders, mothers, creatives—who
         are constantly on the move
         - The product is intentionally dual-purpose: functional utility +
         personal style
      - Apple’s recent launch of phone chains further validates demand and
      category momentum.
      - Unlike LookUp’s broad SKU set, Malk

updates.rawText:
Hi, Nik!
Wanted to reach out and see if this could be a fit, we just kicked off our seed raise.
Our blurb:Middle management is broken: 20-30% of payroll ($3T) wasted on coordination. Pandatron augments these roles, starting with fixing the 95% AI pilot failure rate ($28B+). Our AI middle manager talks to 1000s of employees to support their AI workflow integration while informing C-suite strategy. Now per-seat SaaS, will move to charging 5% of EBITDA ROI (Palantir model). $670k ARR from Panasonic, Mitsubishi, KPMG. Zero churn. Negotiating $8.1M Marriott deal. Team with 5 exits (our head of Product had $300M exit to Capgemini). 

Deck: https://docsend.com/view/k8eu868349wrqegj

Our progress in the last month:* The Marriott deal we are negotiating expanded to $8.1M/year, the reseller’s CEO got involved.* Ex-CEO/founder of Idean Risto Lähdesmäki and ex-Head of Design of Idean (also ex-AWS, Intuit) Rit Mishra will join the team in January. Idean was a design firm in the Bay that Risto sold for $300M to Capgemini.* We just acquired a 2nd mid-tier consulting firm reseller - Mercer, and they already submitted the first $215k offer to a client. BTS Group partnership created the FOMO and made them move. We also got to C level of EY, their head of Product will meet us in New York, which is great as consulting partners drive our growth (and exit).
* Round: 1.5M committed and 400k already in the bank, Indeed.com is likely to participate (TBD) and 2 funds could lead. Among money wired our lead pre-seed Sparkmind VC, hi5 Ventures, early WhatsApp employee, the guy who built Google Cloud.

Sincerely,
Dima Syrotkin - LinkedIn
+1 (415) 792-8591

updates.subject: Aviation Investing Opportunity / ~US$700 Million Orderbook / VC-led ~$22 to 30 million Series B / Ampaire
summary: Proven Technology & Regulatory Advantage – FAA has approved Ampaire’s G-1 certification basis; Supplemental Type Certification (STC) path reduces time to market from 9 years down to only 18 months.
Series B Lead by DiamondStream Partners - DiamondStream has deep aerospace contacts and is led by the founders of Volaris, the largest airline in Mexico, and other aviation experts.
Validated Market Demand – $700 million in signed orders and a $5 billion pipeline with global operators and OEMs including UPS, FedEx, Embraer, Deutsche Aircraft, Archer, and Robinson Helicopters.
Revenue Positive with Low Burn Rate – Over $24 million in paid contracts to date with a burn rate under $300K per month, providing a long runway.
Rapid Deployment & Low Risk – Drop-in engine replacement requires minimal changes to training or infrastructure, lowering CapEx compared to new-aircraft alternatives.
Attractive Economics – The Eco Caravan demonstrates up to a 3× increase in profitability and a five-month ROI in commercial operations (Hawaii case study).
Vast Market Opportunity – Initial launch in the $105 billion commuter segment, with extensions into the $270 billion commercial and $80 billion defense markets.
High Barriers to Entry for Competitors – Ampaire’s first-mover advantage, proven flight hours, and growing patent portfolio create a defensible lead in hybrid aviation.
updates.rawText:
Hey Nik,
White Swan Advisors is representing Ampaire, a revenue-positive aviation company with a $700 million orderbook and an innovative hybrid-electric propulsion system. The company is currently completing a VC-led $22–30 million priced Series B round at a $74M pre-money valuation to finance certification, ramp production, and initiate deliveries to launch customers, including major operators like UPS, FedEx, and top regional carriers.
Their proven technology reduces fuel consumption by 50–85%, trims overall operating costs by 20–40%, and provides a quick payback to aircraft operators in less than 12 months. Its drop-in hybrid engines retrofit existing aircraft, requiring no new airport infrastructure and delivering an immediate boost to profitability for operators.
Ampaire’s FAA-approved, accelerated certification plan and operational track record (>30,000 miles flown) secure its position as the first-to-market hybrid aviation company— capturing early share in a $555 billion total addressable market that includes commuter, commercial, and defense sectors.
Key Investment highlights:

- Proven Technology & Regulatory Advantage – FAA has approved Ampaire’s G-1 certification basis; Supplemental Type Certification (STC) path reduces time to market from 9 years down to only 18 months.

- Series B Lead by DiamondStream Partners - DiamondStream has deep aerospace contacts and is led by the founders of Volaris, the largest airline in Mexico, and other aviation experts.

- Validated Market Demand – $700 million in signed orders and a $5 billion pipeline with global operators and OEMs including UPS, FedEx, Embraer, Deutsche Aircraft, Archer, and Robinson Helicopters.

- Revenue Positive with Low Burn Rate – Over $24 million in paid contracts to date with a burn rate under $300K per month, providing a long runway.

- Rapid Deployment & Low Risk – Drop-in engine replacement requires minimal changes to training or infrastructure, lowering CapEx compared to new-aircraft altern

updates.rawText:
Hi Gillian, Parker and team,

Sharing through the updated forecast that we presented Tuesday.
<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1uYo8TiNlkbdpmnuRqNdKJ2DSUk-IvPBDAFC5XmPADUA/edit?gid=1674558903#gid=1674558903>
Adding context here to how it was modeled, but let me know if you have any
questions or prefer to hop on a call to walk through.

The scenarios are all based on the real results we are seeing so far this
year which have been *heavily influenced by the website conversion rate
drop off YoY (-48%) and the CPM increase YoY. *CPMs are largely outside of
our control as these are mainly influenced by how saturated the auction
house is and the audiences we are targeting. The broader reality is that
what worked last year is not working the same way this year. This is partly
due to our product mix/launches and having more competition against our new
hero SKU mouth tape, but also the greater paid media environment. Because
of this, we need to plan within these constraints and work across multiple
levers (conversion rate, AOV, audience/creative strategy, organic + CRM).

We modeled five scenarios, though they consolidate into three main ones:

   - Scenario 1 and 1.5: Assumes performance stays the same as it is today,
   essentially the control case.
      - *CAC comes out around ~$61* through the end of the year and
      generates 21k in new customer orders from Paid channels.
   - Scenario 2 and 2.5: Builds in improvements to efficiency (higher
   conversion rate and AOV),
      - *CAC comes out around ~$53* through the end of the year and
      generates 24k in new customer orders from Paid channels.
   - Scenario 3: Backs into the CAC goal of $42, showing what metrics would
   need to look like to hit it.

A few things that we also assumed and are important to note:

   - All scenarios are built from paid channel metrics only (Meta +
   Google).
   - The “conversion rate” shown here is ad-click-to-purchase conversion
   rate,

updates.rawText:
The Rhode to $1 Billion

*TLDR;* The Rhode acquisition
<https://www.forbes.com/sites/jemimamcevoy/2025/05/30/hailey-biebers-1-billion-rhode-deal-here-are-26-others-who-made-a-fortune-in-beauty/>
signaled two shifts in the industry. 1) In the past, we’ve seen most beauty
brands get acquired after entering brick and mortar retail (i.e., Sephora,
Target, etc) but Rhode showed us that DTC was more important than the
retail expansion. 2) Just 4 years ago, celebrity brands had a negative
connotation in the market. Rhode demonstrated that celebrity brands can be
a huge win if the emphasis is placed on frequency of posting on social
media + influencer relationships.

*Stats:*

   - *Acquired by e.l.f. Beauty for $1B* ($800M upfront + $200M earn‑out).
   Raised $20M.
   - *FY24 DTC revenue: $212M*
   - *EMV: $725–748M*, with roughly ~15% of impressions from Hailey Bieber

------------------------------
🧴 PRODUCT

   - 10-SKU lineup with high-impact daily-use products (Peptide Lip
   Treatment, Glazing Fluid)
   - Products went viral for *hydration, texture, and minimalist skincare
   routines*
   - Created sticky use cases and high repurchase intent through effective,
   shareable formulas

------------------------------
💵 PRICE

   - Positioned as *accessible luxury* at *$16–$38*
   - High perceived value and margin-friendly pricing
   - Encouraged both self-use and gifting, especially in hero categories
   (lip, moisturizer)

------------------------------
🧭 POSITIONING

   - Founder-led with Hailey Bieber as Chief Creative Officer & Head of
   Innovation
   - Deep cultural resonance with Gen Z and Millennial consumers through
   aesthetic branding and authenticity
   - *Demographic insights*:
      - *38% of customers aged 25–34*
      - *68% under 34*, proving resonance with Rhode’s core digital-native
      audience
   - DTC-first strategy allowed for full brand control, clean data, and
   high-margin growth before retail

updates.subject: New institutional LP joins SuperAngel.Fund II
summary: SuperAngel.Fund <http://superangel.fund/> is an early-stage fund led
updates.rawText:
Dear Friends,

﻿

I'm thrilled to share some exciting news with you first, ahead of our
broader announcement: SuperAngel.Fund II <https://www.superangel.vc/memo>
has secured an $800k commitment from Strawberry Tree Management*, a leading
institutional LP and investment advisor to the AngelList Systematic
Fund-of-Funds <https://superangel.docsend.com/view/d3qkvux85wihyrx7>.*

This Fund-of-Funds leverages proprietary data, predictive modeling and
quantitative analysis across 25,000+ funds and 13,000+ startups on
AngelList. *Their strategy is rooted in identifying GPs with the highest
potential to deliver outsized returns, and based on their core performance
metric we are ranked in the top 1%.*

Notably, this Fund-of-Funds is also backed by Sequoia Capital
<https://www.sequoiacap.com/>, Squarepoint
<https://www.squarepoint-capital.com/> (one of the world's largest
quantitative asset managers), Naval Ravikant
<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naval_Ravikant> and others.


Here’s an excerpt from their signed commitment letter
<https://superangel.docsend.com/view/5cfzptg3v2b8yw6v>:


"We are excited to add Ben Zises’s Fund II, a series of SuperAngel.vc, LP
> to the portfolio of our Systematic Fund-of-Funds. *Ben has one of the
> highest scores (out of hundreds of active GPs on the AngelList platform) by
> our preferred predictor for future investment success...Based on these
> figures, we estimate Ben’s markups over baseline score to be better than
> the 99th percentile of the hundreds of GPs on the AngelList platform. Our
> data strongly suggests that Ben’s next fund has an above average chance of
> being an above average venture fund."*  — Abraham Othman PhD, Chief
> Investment Officer at Strawberry Tree Management Company LLC


You can click here to learn more about SuperAngel.Fund II
<https://portal.angellist.com/l/dr/Dckcs-u>


Thank you for your continued support!


Ben Zises

SuperAngel.Fund
